2.1: The word that Isaiah, son of Amos, saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem.
2.2: In the latter days the mountain of the Lord’s house will be established as the highest of the mountains and exalted above the hills, and all nations will stream to it.,
2.3: And many nations will come and say, «Come, let us go up to the mountain of the Lord, to the house of the God of Jacob, he will teach us his ways, and we will walk in his paths.» For out of Zion shall go forth the law, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.
2.4: He will be the arbiter of the peoples and the judge of many nations. They will beat their swords into plowshares and their spears into sickles. Nation will not take up sword against nation, nor will they train for war anymore.
2.5: House of Jacob, come and let us walk in the light of the Lord.
2.6: For you have forsaken your people, the house of Jacob, because they have long ago reached their limit, they practice magic like the Philistines, and they give hands to the sons of foreigners.
2.7: Their country is filled with silver and gold and their treasures are endless, their country is filled with horses and their chariots are countless.
2.8: Their country is filled with idols; they bow down before the work of their hands, before what their fingers have made.
2.9: That is why mortals will be humbled and humankind brought low: no, you will not forgive them.
2.10: Enter into the rock, hide in the dust, before the terror of the Lord and the splendor of his majesty.
2.11: The haughty eyes of mortals will be humbled and the pride of men will be humbled, and the Lord alone will be exalted on that day.
2.12: For the Lord of hosts will one day, against all pride and all height and against all that is exalted, in order to humble it,
2.13: against all the cedars of Lebanon, tall and lofty, and against all the oaks of Bashan,
2.14: against all the high mountains and against all the lofty hills,
2.15: against every magnificent tower and against every strong wall,
2.16: against all the ships of Tarshish and against everything that delights the eye.
2.17: The arrogance of mortals will be humbled and the pride of men will be brought low, and the Lord alone will be exalted on that day.
2.18: And all the idols will disappear.
2.19: And they will enter the caverns of the rocks and the caverns of the earth, before the terror of the Lord and the splendor of his majesty, when he rises to terrify the earth.
2.20: On that day man will throw away his silver idols and his gold idols, which he made to worship, to the rats and the bats,
2.21: to flee into the holes in the stone and into the clefts of the rocks, before the terror of the Lord and the splendor of his majesty, when he rises to terrify the earth.
2.22: Stop trusting in man, in whose nostrils there is only a breath, for what esteem can one have in him?

3.1: For behold, the Lord, God of hosts, is about to withdraw from Jerusalem and Judah all resources and support, all supplies of bread and all supplies of water,
3.2: hero and warrior, judge, prophet and seer, ancient,
3.3: Captain and notable, advisor, skilled craftsman and adept enchanter.
3.4: And I will give them young men as princes, and children will rule over them.
3.5: And the people will rush man against man, each against his neighbor, they will rush forward, the young man against the old man and the man of no account against the most illustrious.
3.6: When a man takes his brother into his father's house, saying, "You have a cloak; be our leader, and let this ruin be under your protection."«
3.7: On that day he will cry out, «I do not want to be a physician; I have neither bread nor a cloak in my house; do not make me a leader of the people.»
3.8: For Jerusalem is reeling and Judah is falling, because their words and their deeds are against the Lord, to defy the gaze of his majesty.
3.9: The look on their faces testifies against them; like Sodom, they proclaim their sin and do not hide it. Woe to them, for they are the authors of their own destruction.
3.10: Tell him that he is happy, for he will eat the fruit of his works.
3.11: Woe to the wicked, evil will befall him, for what his hands have done will be repaid to him.
3.12: My people are oppressed by children, and women rule them. My people, those who lead you astray and ruin the path you should follow.
3.13: The Lord has risen to render justice, and he stands to judge the peoples.
3.14: The Lord enters into judgment with the elders and princes of his people: «You have devoured the vine, the plunder of the poor is in your houses.
3.15: »By what right do you trample my people and crush the faces of the unfortunate?” declares the Lord, God of hosts.,
3.16: The Lord said: Because the daughters of Zion have become proud, they walk with their heads held high, casting glances, they walk on small steps and jingle the rings on their feet,
3.17: The Lord will make the heads of the daughters of Zion bald, and the Lord will uncover their nakedness.
3.18: On that day, the Lord will remove the luxury of rings, suns, and crescents,
3.21: perfume boxes and amulets, rings and nose rings,
3.22: party dresses and wide tunics, coats and bags,
3.23: mirrors and muslins, turbans and mantillas.
3.24: And instead of perfume, there will be rot; instead of a belt, a rope; instead of curly hair, a bald head; instead of a flowing robe, a sack; instead of beauty, the mark of fire.
3.25: Your warriors will fall by the sword and your heroes in battle.
3.26: The gates of Zion will groan and mourn, and she will sit desolate in the dust.

4.1: Seven women will seize one man on that day and say, «We will eat our own bread and wear our own clothes; only allow us to bear your name, and take away our disgrace.»
4.2: On that day, the branch of the Lord will be the ornament and the glory and the fruit of the earth, will be the pride and the adornment of the survivors of Israel.
4.3: And those who remain in Zion and those who are spared in Jerusalem will be called saints, all those whose names are written for life in Jerusalem.
4.4: When the Lord has washed away the filthiness of the daughters of Zion and cleansed Jerusalem of the blood that is in her midst, by the spirit of judgment and by the spirit of destruction,
4.5: Then the Lord will create over all Mount Zion and its assemblies a cloud by day and smoke and the brightness of a blazing flame by night. For over all his glory there will be a canopy,
4.6: and there will be a tent to provide shade from the heat of the day and to serve as a refuge and shelter from the storm and rain.

5.1: I will sing for my beloved the song of my beloved about his vineyard. My beloved had a vineyard on a fertile hillside.
5.2: He dug up the soil, removed the stones, and planted it with exquisite vines. He built a tower in the middle and also dug a winepress there. He expected it to bear grapes, but it produced sour grapes.
5.3: «And now, you inhabitants of Jerusalem and men of Judah, judge, I pray you, between me and between my vineyard.”.
5.4: What more could I have done for my vineyard that I haven't already done for it? Why did I wait for it to bear grapes, and it only produced sour grapes?
5.5: «And now I will tell you what I am going to do to my vineyard: I will tear down its hedge and it will be grazed, I will break down its wall and it will be trampled underfoot.
5.6: I will make it a desert, and it will no longer be pruned or cultivated; briers and thorns will grow there, and I will command the clouds to no longer let rain fall on it.»
5.7: For the vineyard of the Lord of hosts is the house of Israel, and the men of Judah are the plant he cherished; he looked for justice from them, but behold, bloodshed, righteousness, and a cry of distress.
5.8: Woe to those who add house to house, who join field to field, until there is no more space and they dwell alone in the midst of the land.
5.9: The Lord of hosts spoke this word in my ears: Yes, these many houses will be deserted, large and beautiful, they will no longer have inhabitants.
5.10: For ten acres of vineyards will produce only one bath, a homer of seed will produce only one ephah.
5.11: Woe to those who run after intoxicating drinks from the morning and who, in the evening, prolong their orgy, heated by wine.
5.12: The harp and the lute, the tambourine, the flute and wine, these are their feasts, but they do not regard the work of the Lord and they do not see the work of his hands.
5.13: That is why my people will go into exile without realizing it, its nobility will become a starving troop and its multitude will wither with thirst.
5.14: That is why Sheol expands and opens its mouth without measure; the splendor of Zion descends into it, with its noisy and joyful multitude.
5.15: Mortals will be humbled, men will be brought low, and the eyes of the proud will be brought low.
5.16: And the Lord of hosts will appear great in judgment, and the holy God will appear holy in justice.
5.17: Sheep will graze as if in their pasture, and strangers will devour the devastated fields of the rich.
5.18: Woe to those who draw iniquity with cords of lies and sin as with the reins of a chariot.
5.19: They say, «Let him hurry, let him hasten his work, so that we may see it. Let him come near and carry out the decree of the Holy One of Israel, so that we may know.»
5.20: Woe to those who call evil good and good evil, who make darkness light and light darkness, who make sweet bitter and bitter sweet.
5.21: Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes and intelligent in their own minds.
5.22: Woe to those who are heroes for drinking wine and valiant for mixing strong liquors.
5.23: To those who justify the wicked for a gift and who deprive the righteous of their rights.
5.24: Therefore, as the tongue of fire devours the stubble and as dry grass is consumed by the flame, so their root will be like rot and their flower will be swept away like dust, because they have rejected the law of the Lord of hosts and despised the word of the Holy One of Israel.
5.25: Therefore the Lord's anger burned against his people; he stretched out his hand against them and struck them. The mountains trembled, their corpses lay in the middle of the roads like refuse. Yet his anger was not turned away, and his hand remained stretched out.
5.26: He raises a banner for the distant nations, he whistles to them from the ends of the earth. And behold, they come swift and light.
5.27: Not one of them is weary or faltering, not one is slumbering or sleeping; the belt around their waist is not loosened, nor the strap of their sandals broken.
5.28: Their arrows are sharp, their bows are all strung, the hooves of their horses are as hard as flint, the wheels of their chariots like a hurricane.
5.29: Their roar is that of the lion, they roar like the lion cub: it growls and seizes its prey, it carries it off and no one can snatch it away.
5.30: At that time, there will be a rumbling upon the people, like the rumbling of the sea. They will look upon the land and behold, darkness, anguish, and light. Then night will spread over the sky of the land.

6.1: In the year that King Uzziah died, I saw the Lord sitting on a high and lofty throne, and the train of his robe filled the temple.
6.2: Seraphim stood before him, each with six wings: with two they covered their faces, with two they covered their feet, and with two they flew.
6.3: And they cried out to one another, saying, «Holy, holy, holy is the Lord of hosts; the whole earth is full of his glory.»
6.4: The foundations of the doors shook at the sound of the shouting, and the house filled with smoke.
6.5: Then I said, «Woe to me! I am lost, for I am a man of defiled lips, and I dwell among a people of defiled lips, and my eyes have seen the King, the Lord of hosts.»
6.6: But one of the Seraphim flew towards me, holding in his hand a burning coal, which he had taken from the altar with tongs.
6.7: He touched my mouth with it and said, "See, this has touched your lips; your iniquity is taken away and your sin atoned for."«
6.8: And I heard the voice of the Lord saying, «Whom shall I send, and who will go for us?» And I said, «Here I am, send me.»
6.9: He said, «Go and tell this people: ‘Hear but do not understand, see but do not perceive.’.
6.10: "Harden the hearts of this people, harden their ears, and close their eyes, so that they cannot see with their eyes or hear with their ears, nor turn back and be healed."»
6.11: And I said, «How long, Lord?» He answered, «Until the cities are devastated and without inhabitants, until the houses are without people, and the land is ravaged and desolate,
6.12: until the Lord has removed the men and the land is desolate.
6.13: And if even a tenth of its inhabitants remain, it too will be destroyed. But, just as the sacred tree and the oak, when they are cut down, retain their stump, so the trunk of Israel will be a holy seed.»

7.1: It came to pass in the days of Ahaz, son of Jotham, son of Uzziah, king of Judah, that Rezin, king of Syria, with Phacaeus, son of Romélie, king of Israel, came up against Jerusalem to attack it, but they could not take it.
7.2: The news was announced to the house of David: «Syria is encamped in Ephraim.» Then the heart of the king and the heart of his people trembled as the trees of the forest tremble in the wind.
7.3: And the Lord said to Isaiah, «Go out to meet Ahaz, you and Shear-Jasub, your son, at the end of the canal of the upper pool, on the road to the Fuller’s Field.
7.4: And you will say to him: Beware, keep calm, do not be afraid and do not let your heart fail before these two smoking embers, because of the fury of Rasin and Syria and the son of Romélie.
7.5: Because Syria, Ephraim, and Romélie's son have plotted evil against you, saying:
7.6: «Let us go up against Judah, let us terrify it, let us invade it and install the son of Tabeel as king over it,
7.7: Thus says the Lord God: It shall not have effect, it shall not be.
7.8: For the head of Syria is Damascus and the head of Damascus is Rasin, and in another sixty-five years Ephraim will have ceased to be a people.
7.9: And the head of Ephraim is Samaria, and the head of Samaria is the son of Romélie. If you do not believe, you will not stand.»
7.10: The Lord spoke again to Ahaz, saying:
7.11: «Ask the Lord your God for a sign; ask him in the depths of Sheol or in the heights of heaven.»
7.12: But Ahaz said, "I will not ask, I will not test the Lord."«
7.13: And Isaiah said: «Listen, house of David: Is it too little for you to weary men, that you weary my God also?
7.14: Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign: Behold, the Virgin has conceived and bears a son, and calls him Emmanuel.
7.15: He will eat cream and honey until he knows how to reject evil and choose good.
7.16: For before the child knows how to reject evil and choose good, the land whose two kings you fear will be devastated.
7.17: The Lord will bring upon you, upon your people, and upon your father’s house, days such as have not come since the day Ephraim separated from Judah, the king of Assyria.»
7.18: On that day the Lord will whistle for the fly that is at the end of the rivers of Egypt and for the bee that is in the land of Assyria.
7.19: They will come and settle all of them in the steep valleys and in the crevices of the rocks, on all the bushes and on all the pastures.
7.20: On that day the Lord will shave with a razor that he will have hired beyond the River with the king of Assyria, the head and the hair of the feet, and he will also remove the beard.
7.21: On that day, a man will feed one cow and two sheep
7.22: and, because of the abundance of milk they will give, they will eat nothing but cream, for it is cream and honey that all those who remain in the country will eat.
7.23: On that day, every place where there were a thousand vines, worth a thousand pieces of silver, will be covered with thorns and briers.
7.24: They will enter it with arrows and with bows, for the whole land will be full of thorns and briers.
7.25: And on all the mountains that were cultivated with the hoe, you will no longer go, for fear of brambles and thorns; they will become a pasture for cattle and a land trampled by sheep.

8.1: And the Lord said to me, «Take a large tablet and write on it in characters that all can read: Hasten the plunder. Gather quickly.»
8.2: And I took with me trustworthy witnesses, Uriah the priest and Zechariah, son of Jebarachiah.
8.3: And I approached the prophetess and she conceived and gave birth to a son. And the Lord said to me, «Name him Maher-Schalal-Chasch-Baz.
8.4: For before the child can cry out, »My father, my mother,” the wealth of Damascus and the spoils of Samaria will be brought before the king of Assyria.»
8.5: The Lord spoke to me again, saying:
8.6: «Because this people has despised the gently flowing waters of Siloam and rejoices over Rasin and Romélie’s son,
8.7: Because of this, the Lord is about to bring upon them the mighty, deep waters of the river, the king of Assyria and all his power. It will rise above its banks and overflow all its shores.,
8.8: It will penetrate Judah, it will overflow, it will flood, it will rise up to the neck, and the spreading of its wings will cover the whole extent of your land, O Immanuel.»
8.9: Raise war cries, peoples, and you will be defeated. Listen closely, all you ends of the earth. Arm yourselves, and you will be defeated. Arm yourselves, and you will be defeated.
8.10: Form plans and they will be thwarted, give orders and they will remain without effect, for God is with us.
8.11: For thus the Lord spoke to me, when his hand seized me and he warned me not to follow the way of this people, saying:
8.12: «"Do not call conspiracy everything that this people calls conspiracy, do not fear what they fear, and do not be frightened.".
8.13: The Lord of hosts, it is he whom you shall sanctify, he whom you shall fear and dread.
8.14: And it will be a sanctuary, but also a stumbling block and a rock of offense to both houses of Israel, a snare and a trap to the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
8.15: Many of them will stumble, they will fall and be broken, they will be entangled and captured.
8.16: "Bind the testimony, seal the teaching in the hearts of my disciples."»
8.17: I hope in the Lord, who hides his face from the house of Jacob, and I trust in him.
8.18: Behold, I and my children whom God has given me are signs and wonders in Israel from the Lord of hosts, who dwells on Mount Zion.
8.19: When they say to you, «Consult those who summon the dead and the soothsayers who whisper and murmur,» answer, «Should not a people consult their God? Should they consult the dead for the living?”
8.20: "To teaching and to bearing witness." If the people speak a different language, there is no dawn for them.
8.21: He will wander through the land, afflicted with misery and hunger, and when he is hungry, he will curse his king and his God in his fury.
8.22: He will lift up his eyes on high and lower them to the earth: and behold, distress, darkness, and gloomy anguish; he will be cast down into darkness.
8.23: But there is no more darkness for the land that has been in anguish. As the first time brought shame upon the land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, so the last time will bring glory to the Way of the Sea, to the land beyond the Jordan, and to the district of the nations.

9.1: The people who walked in darkness have seen a great light, and on those who dwelt in the land of the shadow of death, a light has dawned.
9.2: You have multiplied your people, you have made their joy great; they rejoice before you as people rejoice at the harvest, as people shout when dividing the spoils.
9.3: For the yoke that weighed upon him, the bar that struck his shoulder, the staff of his oppressor, you have broken as on the day of Midian.
9.4: For every sandal of the warrior in the melee and every cloak rolled in blood are given over to the fire, the fire devours them.
9.5: For to us a child is born, to us a son is given; and the government is placed upon his shoulders, and his name is called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace:
9.6: To extend the empire and to give endless peace to the throne of David and to his kingship, to establish and uphold it in justice and righteousness, now and forever. The zeal of the Lord of hosts will accomplish this.
9.7: The Lord sent a word to Jacob, and it fell upon Israel.,
9.8: All the people will know about it, Ephraim and the inhabitants of Samaria, who say in their pride and in the pride of their hearts:
9.9: «"The bricks have fallen, let us build with hewn stone; the sycamores are cut down, let us replace them with cedars."»
9.10: The Lord raises up against them the adversaries of Rasin and he goads their enemies.,
9.11: Syria to the east, the Philistines to the west, and they will devour Israel with gusto. Despite all this, his anger has not turned away, and his hand remains outstretched.
9.12: For the people did not return to the One who struck them, nor did they seek the Lord of hosts.
9.13: And the Lord will cut off from Israel the head and the tail, the palm and the rush, in a single day.
9.14: The ancient and noble man is the head, and the prophet who teaches lies is the tail.
9.15: Those who guide this people lead them astray, and those who are guided are lost.
9.16: Therefore the Lord will not be pleased with his young men, nor will he have compassion on his fatherless children and widows, for they are all ungodly and wicked, and every mouth speaks ungodliness. For all this his anger has not turned away, and his hand remains stretched out.
9.17: For malice has been kindled like a fire, it devours brambles and thorns, it sets the thickets of the forest ablaze, and the smoke rises in swirling eddies.
9.18: By the wrath of the Lord of hosts, the land is ablaze and the people have become prey to the flames. No one spares his brother.
9.19: We cut to the right and are hungry, we devour to the left and are not satisfied, each one devours the flesh of his own arm, Manasseh against Ephraim, Ephraim against Manasseh, both together against Judah.
9.20: Despite all this, his anger did not subside and his hand remained outstretched.

10.1: Woe to those who pronounce unjust judgments and to the scribes who write unjust sentences,
10.2: excluding the weak from the court and depriving the afflicted of my people of their rights, making widows their prey and plundering orphans.
10.3: What will you do on the day of the visitation and in the catastrophe that will come from afar? To whom will you flee for help and where will you deposit your treasures?
10.4: All that remains is to bow down among the captives, or to fall among the slain. Despite all this, his anger has not turned away, and his hand remains outstretched.
10.5: Woe to Assur, instrument of my anger! The staff in his hand is the instrument of my fury!,
10.6: I send him against an ungodly nation, I give him my orders against the people of my wrath, to plunder them and take spoils and trample them underfoot like the mud of the streets.
10.7: But he does not see it that way, nor is that the thought of his heart, for he only seeks to destroy and exterminate nations, not in small numbers.
10.8: He said, "Are not all my princes kings?"
10.9: Was it not the case with Calno as with Carchemish, and with Hamath as with Arphad, and with Samaria as with Damascus?
10.10: Just as my hand reached the realms of the powerless gods, whose idols surpassed those of Jerusalem and Samaria, as I did to Samaria and its gods,
10.11: "Should I not do the same to Jerusalem and its images?"»
10.12: But it will happen: When the Lord has finished all his work, on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem: «I will punish the fruit of the proud heart of the king of Assyria and the arrogance of his haughty eyes.»
10.13: For he said: «By the strength of my hand I have done this, and by my wisdom, for I am understanding. I have moved the boundaries of the peoples, I have plundered their treasures, and like a mighty man I have overthrown from the throne those who sat on it.”.
10.14: My hand has grasped, like a nest, the riches of the peoples, and, as one gathers abandoned eggs, I have gathered all the earth, without anyone having stirred a wing, opened a beak, or uttered a cry.»
10.15: Does the axe boast against the hand that wields it, does the saw exalt itself against the one who moves it? As if the wooden rod could move the one who lifts it, as if the stick could lift what is not wood.
10.16: Therefore the Lord God of hosts will send decay upon his strong warriors, and under his magnificence a fire will ignite, like the fire of a conflagration.
10.17: The light of Israel will be a fire and its Holy One a flame, which will consume and devour its thorns and briers in a single day.
10.18: And the glory of his forest and his orchard, he will annihilate it from soul to body, it will be like a sick person dying of consumption.
10.19: The remaining trees in his forest could be counted; a child would write them down.
10.20: And it will happen on that day: The remnant of Israel and the survivors of the house of Jacob will no longer rely on the one who struck them, but they will rely on the Lord, the Holy One of Israel, with faithfulness.
10.21: A remnant will return, a remnant of Jacob, to the mighty God.
10.22: For though your people, O Israel, should be like the sand of the sea, they are but a remnant that will return; destruction is decreed, and justice will overflow.
10.23: For the destruction that he has decreed, the Lord God of hosts will accomplish throughout the land.
10.24: Therefore this is what the Lord God of hosts says: «Do not be afraid of Assyria, O my people who dwell in Zion, when he strikes you with the club and lifts up the rod against you, as Egypt did of old.
10.25: For, in a very short time, my wrath will cease and my anger will turn against them to destroy them.
10.26: And the Lord of hosts will raise his whip against them, as he struck Midian at the rock of Oreb and as his staff was over the sea, and he will raise it, as he did in Egypt of old.
10.27: And it will happen on that day: The burden of Assur will be removed from your shoulder and his yoke from off your neck, and your strength will break the yoke.
10.28: He came to Ajath, he passed through Magron, he left his luggage at Machmas.
10.29: They passed the parade, they camped the night at Gaba, Rama is terrified, Saul's Gaba flees.
10.30: Cry out in distress, daughter of Gallim. Listen, Laïs. Poor Anathoth.
10.31: Medmena is scattering, the inhabitants of Gabim are fleeing.
10.32: In one more day he will be at Nobah, he will raise his hand against the mountain of the daughter of Zion, against the hill of Jerusalem.
10.33: Behold, the Lord, God of hosts, strikes down the branches of the trees with a roar; the tallest are cut down, the highest are thrown to the ground.
10.34: The thickets of the forest are cut with iron and Lebanon falls under the blows of a Mighty One.

11.1: A branch will spring from the stump of Jesse, and from his roots a shoot will grow.
11.2: The Spirit of the Lord will rest on him, the Spirit of wisdom and understanding, the Spirit of counsel and might, the Spirit of knowledge and the fear of the Lord.
11.3: He will find his delight in the fear of the Lord. He will not judge by what he sees with his eyes, nor decide by what he hears.
11.4: He will judge the lowly with righteousness and decide with justice for the meek of the earth. He will strike the earth with the rod of his word and with the breath of his lips he will slay the wicked.
11.5: Justice will be girded around her sides and faithfulness will be the belt around her waist.
11.6: The wolf will live with the lamb, the leopard will rest with the kid, the calf, the lion, and the fattened ox will live together, and a little child will lead them.
11.7: The cow and the bear will go to the same pasture, their young will have the same shelter, and the lion will eat fodder like the ox.
11.8: The infant will frolic on the viper's hole and in the basilisk's lair the barely weaned child will put his hand.
11.9: They will neither harm nor destroy on all my holy mountain, for the land will be filled with the knowledge of the Lord, as the waters cover the sea.
11.10: And it will happen on that day: The root of Jesse, raised as a banner for the peoples, will be sought by the nations, and his dwelling place will be glorious.
11.11: And it will happen on that day: the Lord will stretch out his hand a second time to redeem the remnant of his people, those who will remain in the lands of Assyria and Egypt, Patros, Ethiopia, Elam, Sennacherib, Hamath, and the islands of the sea.
11.12: He will raise a banner for the nations and gather the exiles of Israel, he will assemble the scattered people of Judah from the four corners of the earth.
11.13: Ephraim's jealousy will disappear and Judah's enmities will cease; Ephraim will no longer be jealous of Judah and Judah will no longer be Ephraim's enemy.
11.14: They will swoop down on the Philistines' shoulders in the West, they will plunder the sons of the East together, they will lay hands on Edom and Moab, and the sons of Ammon will be subject to them.
11.15: The Lord will strike the tongue of the sea of Egypt with an apostate, and he will raise his hand against the River, with the strength of his breath, and, striking it, he will divide it into seven streams, and make it a place to walk in sandals.
11.16: And so there will be a way for the remnant of his people, who will survive in the land of Assyria, just as there was one for Israel on the day they came up from the land of Egypt.

12.1: And you will say on that day: I praise you, Lord, for you were angry, but your anger has turned away and you comfort me.
12.2: Here is the God of my salvation; I trust and I am not afraid, for the Lord is my strength and my praise. The Lord has become my salvation.
12.3: You will draw water with joy from the springs of salvation,
12.4: And you will say on that day: Praise the Lord, call upon his name, publish among the peoples his great works, proclaim that his name is exalted.
12.5: Sing to the Lord, for he has done magnificent things, let it be known throughout the earth.
12.6: Shout aloud, exult, inhabitant of Zion, for the Holy One of Israel is great in your midst.

13.1: The oracle concerning Babylon, revealed to Isaiah, son of Amoz.
13.2: On a bare mountain raise a banner, call them aloud, wave your hands and let them pass through the gates of princes.
13.3: I have given orders to my consecrated ones, I have called my heroes to serve my wrath, those who joyfully acclaim my majesty.
13.4: A rumbling is heard on the mountains: it sounds like the noise of a great people, like the tumult of kingdoms and nations gathered together: It is the Lord of hosts reviewing his war troops.
13.5: They come from a distant land, from the ends of the earth, the Lord and the instruments of his wrath, to ravage the whole earth.
13.6: Shout, for the day of the Lord is near: it comes like a devastation from the Almighty.
13.7: That is why every hand will fail and every human heart will melt.
13.8: They will tremble, they will be seized by trances and pains, they will writhe like a woman giving birth, they will look at each other in astonishment, their faces will be like flames.
13.9: Behold, the day of the Lord has come, a cruel day of fury and fierce anger, to make the earth a desolate place and to destroy its sinners.
13.10: For the stars of heaven and their constellations do not give their light, the sun is darkened at its rising and the moon no longer sheds its light.
13.11: I will punish the world for its malice and the wicked for their iniquity; I will put an end to the pride of the insolent and I will humble the arrogance of tyrants.
13.12: I will make men rarer than fine gold, rarer than the gold of Ophir.
13.13: Therefore I will make the heavens tremble and the earth shake from its place, by the fury of the Lord of hosts, on the day when his anger is kindled.
13.14: Then, like a gazelle that is being chased, like a flock that no one gathers, each will turn to his own people and flee to his own country.
13.15: All those who are found will be pierced through, all those who are seized will fall by the sword.
13.16: Their little children will be crushed before their eyes, their homes looted, and their wives raped.
13.17: Behold, I am raising up against them the Medes, who do not value silver and do not covet gold.
13.18: Their bows will crush the young men, they will show no mercy to the fruit of the womb, their eyes will have no pity on children.
13.19: And Babylon, the ornament of kingdoms, the adornment of the proud Chaldeans, will be like Sodom and Gomorrah, which God destroyed.
13.20: It will never again be populated, it will never again be inhabited in the course of the ages, the Arab will not pitch his tent there and the shepherd will not pen his flocks there.
13.21: Desert animals will make their home there, owls will fill its houses, ostriches will live there, and the goat will leap there.
13.22: Jackals will howl in her deserted palaces and wild dogs in her pleasure houses. Her time is near and her days will not be prolonged.

14.1: For the Lord will have mercy on Jacob and will again choose Israel, and will restore them in their own land; foreigners will join them and attach themselves to the house of Jacob.
14.2: The peoples will take them and bring them back to their homes, and the house of Israel will possess them in the land of the Lord as male and female servants; they will take captive those who had taken them captive, and they will rule over their oppressors.
14.3: And on the day when the Lord gives you rest from your labor, from your anxieties, and from the harsh bondage that was imposed upon you,
14.4: You will sing this satire against the king of Babylon and you will say: How did the tyrant end, how did the oppression cease?
14.5: The Lord has broken the heel of the wicked, the scepter of the rulers.
14.6: He struck the peoples with fury, with relentless blows; in his anger, he held the nations under the yoke with unceasing persecution.
14.7: The whole earth is at rest, it is quiet, it bursts forth in cries of joy.
14.8: Even the cypresses rejoice at your fall, along with the cedars of Lebanon: "Since you have lain there, no one comes up to cut us down."«
14.9: Sheol in its depths is stirred for you, to come to meet you, it awakens the shades for you, all the monarchs of the earth, it raises from their thrones all the kings of the nations.
14.10: They all speak up to tell you: "You too have fallen like us, and now you are like us."«
14.11: Your splendor has descended to Sheol, with the sound of your harps; worms are spread beneath you, and vermin are your covering.
14.12: How you have fallen from heaven, morning star, son of the dawn! How you have been cast down to the earth, you who once laid low the nations!
14.13: You who said in your heart, «I will ascend to the heavens, above the stars of God; I will raise my throne; I will sit enthroned on the mount of assembly, on the heights of the north,
14.14: I will ascend to the tops of the clouds; I will be like the Most High.»
14.15: And you have descended to Sheol, to the depths of the abyss.
14.16: Those who see you fix their gaze upon you, they consider you attentively: «Is this the man who troubled the earth, who shook kingdoms,
14.17: who turned the world into a desert, destroyed cities, and did not allow his captives to return home?
14.18: All the kings of the nations, all of them, rest with honor, each in his own house,
14.19: But you were cast out of your tomb like a despised branch, covered with slain, slaughtered by the sword, and hurled down the rocky sides of the abyss, like a corpse trampled underfoot.
14.20: You will not be with them in the grave because you have ruined your country, you have caused the destruction of your people. The race of the wicked will never be named again.
14.21: Prepare a massacre for his sons, for the crime of their fathers. May they not rise again to conquer the land, may they not cover the face of the world with cities.
14.22: I will rise up against them, declares the Lord of hosts, and I will destroy from Babylon the name and the remnant, the offspring and the descendants, declares the Lord.
14.23: And I will make it a nest of hedgehogs and a pond of waters, and I will sweep it away with the broom of destruction, declares the Lord of hosts.
14.24: The Lord of hosts has sworn, saying: «Yes, the purpose which is fixed shall be accomplished and what I have decided shall be accomplished.
14.25: I will crush Assyria in my land and trample it on my mountains. Then its yoke will be removed from them and its burden lifted from their shoulders.
14.26: This is the plan that is determined against the whole earth, and this is the hand that is stretched out against all nations.
14.27: For the Lord of hosts has decided, and who can stop him? His hand is outstretched, and who can turn it back?»
14.28: In the year of King Ahaz's death, this oracle was pronounced:
14.29: Do not rejoice, all you Philistia, that the rod that struck you has been broken, for from the serpent’s offspring will come a basilisk, and its fruit will be a flying dragon.
14.30: Then the poorest will find their pasture and the unfortunate will rest in safety, but I will cause your race to perish by hunger and what remains of you will be exterminated.
14.31: Howl, O gate! Cry out, O city! Swoon with fear, all of Philistia! For smoke comes from the north, and not one of his battalions deserts.
14.32: What will be said to the envoys of the nation? That the Lord has founded Zion and that the afflicted of his people find refuge there.

15.1: Oracle concerning Moab. Yes, on the night it was sacked, Ar-Moab was ruined. On the night it was sacked, Kir-Moab was ruined.
15.2: They go up to the temple of Chamos and to Dibon, to the high places, to weep, over Nebo and Medeba, Moab laments, every head is shaved, every beard is cut off.
15.3: In its streets, they cover themselves with bags, on its roofs and in its public squares, they all shout and burst into tears.
15.4: Hesebon and Elealeh raise their voices; their voices are heard as far as Jahas. Therefore the warriors of Moab wail, and his soul trembles.
15.5: My heart groans for Moab; its defenders are already at Segor, at Eglath-shelishiyah. Yes, the ascent of Luhith is climbed with weeping, and on the road to Horonaim, cries of distress are raised.
15.6: For the waters of Nimrim have dried up, the grass is withered, the turf is destroyed, there is no more greenery.
15.7: What they were able to save and their supplies, they transported beyond the Willow Torrent.
15.8: For the cries have gone around the territory of Moab, its wailings resound as far as Eglaim, its wailings as far as Beer-Elim
15.9: because the waters of Dimon are full of blood, for I will bring upon Dimon an additional calamity, a lion upon the survivors of Moab, upon what will remain in the land.

16.1: «"Send the lamb of the ruler of the land, of Petra, across the desert to the mountain of the daughter of Zion."»
16.2: Like fleeing birds, like a scattered nest, so are the daughters of Moab at the fords of the Arnon:
16.3: «"Advise us, be our arbiter, give us shade, as in the night, in the middle of the day, hide those who are being pursued, do not betray fugitives.
16.4: Let the fugitives of Moab dwell with you, be their refuge from the destroyer, for the invasion has ceased, the devastation has ended, the oppressors have disappeared from the land.
16.5: The throne is established by mercy, and on this throne, in the tent of David, will sit a judge who pursues justice and is zealous for righteousness.»
16.6: «We know the pride of Moab, the very proud, his pride and his arrogance, his arrogance and his lying speech.»
16.7: Let Moab wail for Moab, let all wail. Over the raisin cakes of Kir-Hareseth, groan in utter dismay.
16.8: For the fields of Hezebon are desolate, the vine of Sabamah has been destroyed by the rulers of the nations, the vines which stretched as far as Jazer, which went on to be lost in the desert, which sent out their shoots far away, which passed beyond the sea.
16.9: That is why I weep, as Jazer weeps, over the vineyard of Sabama, I water you with my tears, Hesebon, Elealeh, for on your fruits and on your harvest, the cry of the winepresser has come to melt.
16.10: Joy and merriment have disappeared from the orchards, in the vineyards, no more songs, no more cries of joy, the grape harvester no longer treads the wine in the vats, I have silenced the joyful cry of the presser.
16.11: Therefore my bowels tremble like a harp concerning Moab, and my heart concerning Kir-Hares.
16.12: And we will see Moab wear himself out on his high places, he will enter his sanctuary to pray, and he will obtain nothing.
16.13: This is the oracle that the Lord pronounced concerning Moab long ago.
16.14: And now the Lord speaks and says: «In three years, counted as the years of a hired servant, the glory of Moab with its great multitude will be debased, and what remains of it will be little, small, and weak.».

17.1: The oracle concerning Damascus: Damascus is removed from the number of cities; it will become nothing but a heap of ruins.
17.2: The towns of Aroër are abandoned, they are given over to the herds, they rest there and nobody chases them away.
17.3: The fortress will be taken from Ephraim and the kingdom from Damascus; the remnant of Syria will be like the glory of the children of Israel, declares the Lord of hosts.
17.4: And it will happen on that day: Jacob’s glory will fade and his body fat will be wasted away.
17.5: It will be like when the reaper gathers the sheaves and his arm cuts the ears of grain, it will be like when the ears of grain are gathered in the valley of Raphaim.
17.6: There will be a gleaning left, as when one shakes the olive tree, two or three olives at the top of the tree, four or five on the branches of the tree, declares the Lord, the God of Israel.
17.7: On that day, man will look to his Maker and his eyes will behold the Holy One of Israel.
17.8: He will no longer look towards the altars, the work of his hands, he will no longer contemplate what his fingers have fashioned, the sacred poles and the pillars of the Sun.
17.9: On that day, its fortified cities will be like the abandoned cities in the woods and mountains, which were abandoned before the children of Israel; it will be a desert.
17.10: For you have forgotten the God of your salvation, you have not remembered the Rock of your strength. Therefore you plant pleasant crops and put in them foreign vines.
17.11: The day you plant them, you surround them with a fence, and the next morning you make your seeds flower, and the harvest escapes you on the day of sickness and mortal pain.
17.12: Oh. Murmur of many peoples. They roar like the roar of the seas. Rumble of nations. They roar like the roar of mighty waters.
17.13: The nations roar like the roaring of great waters. But he rebukes them: they flee far away, they are scattered, like straw on the heights before the wind, like a whirlwind of dust before the hurricane.
17.14: In the evening comes consternation, before morning they are no more. This is the portion of those who plunder us and the fate of those who rob us.

18.1: Oh, land where the rustling of wings resounds, beyond the rivers of Cush,
18.2: You who send messengers across the sea, in papyrus boats, across the face of the waters. Go, swift messengers, to the nation of great stature, of shaven skin, to the people feared far away, an imperious and crushing nation whose land is crisscrossed by rivers.
18.3: All you inhabitants of the world, inhabitants of the earth, when the banner is raised on the mountains, look, when the trumpet sounds, listen.
18.4: For thus the Lord said to me: «I will sit at rest and watch, as I sit in my dwelling, like the warmth of a bright sun, like a cloud of dew in the heat of harvest.»
18.5: For before the harvest, when the flowering is over and the flower has become a soon-to-be-ripe cluster, then he will cut the shoots with a sickle, he will remove the shoots, he will detach them.
18.6: They will all be given over together to the mountain vultures and to the beasts of the earth; the vultures will make prey of them during the summer and the beasts of the earth during the winter.
18.7: At that time an offering will be brought to the Lord of hosts from the tall, shaven-skinned people, from the people feared far away, an imperious and crushing nation whose land is crisscrossed with rivers, to the dwelling place of the name of the Lord of hosts, to Mount Zion.

19.1: The oracle concerning Egypt. Behold, the Lord, borne on a light cloud, enters Egypt; the idols of Egypt tremble before him, and the heart of Egypt melts within her.
19.2: I will push Egypt against Egypt, and they will fight brother against brother, friend against friend, city against city, kingdom against kingdom.
19.3: The spirit of Egypt will vanish within her and I will destroy her council; they will consult the idols and the enchanters, the necromancers and the soothsayers.
19.4: I will deliver Egypt into the hands of a harsh master, and a fearsome king will rule over them, declares the Lord, God of hosts.
19.5: The waters of the sea will dry up, the river will run dry and run dry.
19.6: The rivers will become foul, the canals of Egypt will lower and dry up, the rushes and reeds will wither.
19.7: The meadows along the Nile, on the banks of the Nile, all the sown fields along the Nile, will dry up, vanish, and there will be none left.
19.8: The fishermen will groan and wail, all who cast their hooks into the river, those who spread their nets across the face of the waters, will be desolate.
19.9: Those who work with combed flax and those who weave cotton will be dismayed.
19.10: The pillars of Egypt will be broken, all the craftsmen will be in despair.
19.11: The princes of Tanis are nothing but fools, the wise advisors of Pharaoh whose counsel is foolish. How dare you say to Pharaoh, "I am the son of the wise, the son of ancient kings?"«
19.12: Where are your wise men? Let them tell you, let them discover what the Lord of hosts has decreed against Egypt.
19.13: The princes of Tanis have lost their way, the princes of Memphis are deluded, they are leading Egypt astray, they, the cornerstone of its castes.
19.14: The Lord has poured out a spirit of confusion in the midst of her, and they make Egypt wander in all that she does, as a drunken man wanders in his vomit.
19.15: And there will be no work that will benefit Egypt, from whatever the head or the tail, the palm or the rush, may do.
19.16: On that day, Egypt will be like the women; she will tremble and be terrified when she sees the hand of the Lord of hosts raised against her.
19.17: And the land of Judah will be a source of terror for Egypt, whenever it is brought to her attention, she will tremble because of the decree of the Lord of hosts, which he has pronounced against her.
19.18: On that day there will be five cities in the land of Egypt that will speak the language of Canaan and will pledge allegiance to the Lord of hosts; one of them will be called City of the Sun.
19.19: On that day there will be an altar to the Lord in the middle of the land of Egypt and a pillar to the Lord near the border.
19.20: And this will be a signal and a testimony to the Lord of hosts in the land of Egypt, when they cry out to the Lord because of their oppressors; he will send them a savior and a champion to deliver them.
19.21: The Lord will make himself known to Egypt, and Egypt will know the Lord. On that day they will offer sacrifices and offerings, and they will make vows to the Lord and fulfill them.
19.22: The Lord will strike Egypt, striking and healing. They will turn to the Lord, and he will be moved by their pleas and heal them.
19.23: On that day there will be a highway from Egypt to Assyria, the Assyrian will come to Egypt and the Egyptian will go to Assyria, and Egypt will serve the Lord with Assyria.
19.24: On that day Israel will be united, the third, with Egypt and Assyria, to be a blessing in the midst of the earth.
19.25: The Lord of hosts will bless them, saying: «Blessed be Egypt my people, and Assyria the work of my hands, and Israel my inheritance.»

20.1: The year that Thartan came to Azoth, sent by Sargon, king of Assyria, besieged Azoth and captured it,
20.2: At that time the Lord spoke through Isaiah, son of Amos, saying, «Go, take off the sackcloth that covers your hips and remove the sandals from your feet.» And he did so, walking naked and barefoot.
20.3: And the Lord said, «Just as my servant Isaiah walked naked and barefoot for three years, being a sign and a portent to Egypt and to Ethiopia,
20.4: Thus the king of Assyria will take the captives of Egypt and the deportees of Ethiopia, young men and old men, naked and barefoot and with their buttocks exposed, to the shame of Egypt.
20.5: Then they will be dismayed and confused, because of Ethiopia, which was their hope, and Egypt, which was their source of glory.
20.6: And the inhabitant of that shore will say on that day: "This is what has become of him in whom we hoped, him to whom we wanted to flee for help, to be delivered from the hands of the king of Assyria. How then shall we escape?"»

21.1: Oracle concerning the desert of the sea. As hurricanes pass through the south, this comes from the desert, from a fearsome land.
21.2: A terrible vision was shown to me: the plunderer plunders and the ravager ravages. Go up, Elam. Besiege, Mede. I put an end to all the groaning.
21.3: That is why my loins are filled with anguish, pains seize me, like the pains of a woman in childbirth. I writhe at the hearing, I am terrified at the sight,
21.4: My heart wanders, terror seizes me, the night I desired has been turned into a time of terrors for me.
21.5: The table is set, the sentry stands watch, we eat, we drink. "Stand up, captains. Anoint the shield."«
21.6: For thus the Lord spoke to me: «Go, set up a watchman, and let him report what he sees.
21.7: And she will see riders, two by two, on horses, riders on donkeys, riders on camels. And she will watch them attentively, very attentively.»
21.8: And she cried out like a lion: «On the watchtower, Lord, I stand, continually all day long, and at my post I stand every night.
21.9: "Here comes the cavalry, riders two by two." And she continued, saying, "Fallen, fallen is Babylon, and all the statues of her gods; he has shattered them to the ground."»
21.10: O my wheat that is trodden, grain of my threshing floor, what I have heard from the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, I proclaim to you.
21.11: Oracle on Duma. They call to me from Seir: "Watchman, where is the night? Watchman, where is the night?"«
21.12: The sentry replied: "Morning comes, and so does night. If you wish to question me, question me; come back another time."«
21.13: Oracle in Arabia. You will spend the night in the steppes of Arabia, caravans of Dedan.
21.14: To those who are thirsty, bring water. The inhabitants of the land of Thema offer bread to fugitives.
21.15: For they fled from the sword, from the drawn sword, from the drawn bow, from the terrible battle.
21.16: For this is what the Lord said to me: “In one more year, like the years of a hired servant, all the glory of Cedar will be gone.”
21.17: And of the many bows of the valiant sons of Cedar, little will remain, for the Lord, the God of Israel, has spoken.

22.1: Oracle of the Valley of Vision. What is it that you have gone up entirely onto the rooftops?,
22.2: Noisy city, full of tumult, joyous city? Your dead did not perish by the sword, nor did they fall in battle.
22.3: Your leaders all fled together; they were captured without a bow being fired; all your citizens were captured together as they fled far away.
22.4: That is why I said, «Turn your eyes away from me, lest I weep bitterly. Do not hasten to console me over the ruin of the daughter of my people.»
22.5: For it is a day of confusion, of crushing, of perplexity, sent by the Lord God of hosts, in the valley of visions, the wall is demolished, cries are heard towards the mountain.
22.6: Elam took the quiver, he comes with chariots and horsemen, and Kir drew the shield from its covering.
22.7: Your most beautiful valleys are filled with chariots and horsemen are stationed before your gates; Judah is being veiled.
22.8: And on that day you look towards the arsenal of the forest palace,
22.9: And the breaches in the city of David, you see that they are many. And you have gathered the waters of the lower pool,
22.10: You counted the houses of Jerusalem and you demolished the houses to repair the walls.
22.11: You made a reservoir between the two walls for the waters of the old pond. But you did not look to the One who made it, nor did you see the One who prepared it from afar.
22.12: The Lord God of hosts called you on that day to weep and to mourn, to shave your heads and to put on sackcloth.
22.13: And here is the feast, the rejoicing, they slaughter oxen, they kill sheep, they gorge themselves on meat and drink wine, "Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die."«
22.14: The Lord of hosts has revealed it in my ears: This sin will not be forgiven you until you die, says the Lord, God of hosts.
22.15: Thus spoke the Lord God of hosts: Go to this steward, Sobna, the prefect of the palace, and tell him:
22.16: «"What are you doing here and who are you here, that you are digging yourself a tomb here, you who are digging yourself a tomb on a high place, who are hewing yourself a dwelling in the rock?
22.17: Behold, the Lord is about to hurl you down with a mighty arm,
22.18: He will make you spin, he will make you roll with force, roll like a ball, across a vast plain. There you will die, there will be your magnificent chariots, O reproach to your master's house.
22.19: I will drive you from your post; the Lord will uproot you from your place.»
22.20: And it will happen on that day: I will call my servant Eliacim, son of Helkiah,
22.21: I will clothe him with your tunic and gird him with your sash, I will place your authority in his hands, and he will be a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem and to the house of Judah.
22.22: I will place on his shoulder the key to the house of David; he will open it and no one will shut it, he will shut it and no one will open it.
22.23: I will drive it like a nail into a firm place, and it will become a throne of glory for his father's house.
22.24: To him will hang all the glory of his father's house, the sons and the grandsons, all the smallest vessels, from the cup to the jars.
22.25: On that day, declares the Lord of hosts, the nail driven into a firm place will be pulled out, it will be pulled up and fall, and the burden it carried will be destroyed, for the Lord has spoken.

23.1: Oracle concerning Tyre. Howl, ships of Tarshish, for it is destroyed. No more houses, no more entrance. It is from the land of Cetim that they receive the news.
23.2: Be astonished, inhabitants of the coast, that the merchants of Sidon, traveling the sea, were filled.
23.3: Across the great waters, the wheat of the Nile, the harvests of the river were its income, it was the market of the nations.
23.4: Be ashamed, Sidon, for the sea has spoken, the citadel of the sea, saying: «I have not been in labor, I have not given birth, I have not nourished young men, nor have I brought up virgins.
23.5: When Egypt learns the news, it will be seized with terror at the news of the fall of Tyre.
23.6: Go to Tarshish and howl, inhabitants of the coast.
23.7: Is this your joyous city, whose origins dated back to ancient times and which his feet carried far away to dwell in?
23.8: Who decided this against Tyre, who bestowed crowns, whose merchants were princes and traders the great men of the earth?
23.9: It is the Lord of hosts who has decided this, to put to shame the pride of all that glitters, to humble all the great ones of the earth.
23.10: Spread out in your land, like the Nile, daughter of Tarshish, for you no longer have a girdle.
23.11: The Lord stretched out his hand over the sea, he made the kingdoms tremble, he decreed against Canaan the ruin of its fortresses.
23.12: And he said: «You will no longer rejoice, dishonored virgin, daughter of Sidon. Arise, go to Cetim, there will be no more rest for you.
23.13: »Look at the land of the Chaldeans, this people who did not exist, who gave Assyria over to the beasts of the desert; they raised their towers, destroyed its palaces, and made it a heap of ruins.”
23.14: Howl, ships of Tarsis, for your fortress is destroyed.
23.15: On that day, Tyre will be forgotten for seventy years, the length of a king's reign. And at the end of seventy years, Tyre will be as in the courtesan's song:
23.16: «"Take your harp, wander around the city, forgotten courtesan, play with artistry, multiply your songs, so that you may be remembered."»
23.17: And it will happen, after seventy years, that the Lord will visit Tyre and she will again receive her wages and she will prostitute herself to all the kingdoms of the earth, on the face of the world.
23.18: And his gain and his wages shall be dedicated to the Lord; they shall not be hoarded or stored up, for his gain shall belong to those who dwell before the Lord, that they may eat and be satisfied and adorn themselves magnificently.

24.1: Behold, the Lord is about to devastate the whole earth and depopulate it; he will distort its face and scatter its inhabitants.
24.2: And it will be the same for the priest as for the people, for the master as for his servant, for the mistress as for her maid, for the seller as for the buyer, for the borrower as for the lender, for the debtor as for the creditor.
24.3: The land will be devastated and given over to plunder, for the Lord has spoken this word.
24.4: The earth is in mourning, exhausted, the world is languishing and exhausted, the elite of the earth's inhabitants are languishing.
24.5: The earth was desecrated under its inhabitants because they transgressed the laws, violated the commandment, and broke the everlasting covenant.
24.6: That is why the curse devours the earth and its inhabitants bear their punishment. That is why the inhabitants of the earth are consumed and what remains of mortals is few in number.
24.7: The juice of the vine mourns, the vine languishes, all those who had joy in their hearts groan.
24.8: The joyful sound of the tambourines has ceased, the noisy celebrations have ended, the joyful sound of the harp has ceased.
24.9: We no longer drink wine to the sound of songs; the intoxicating liquor is bitter to the drinker.
24.10: It is overturned, the city is in confusion, every house is closed, no one can enter.
24.11: There are shouts in the streets, for lack of wine, all merriment has disappeared, joy is banished from the earth.
24.12: Nothing remains of the city but rubble and the broken gates are in ruins.
24.13: For it will be in the midst of the earth, among the peoples, as when olives are cut down, as when gleanings are gathered after the grape harvest.
24.14: These are the ones who raise their voices, they sing, they acclaim from the sea the majesty of the Lord:
24.15: «Praise the Lord in the dawn regions, the name of the Lord, the God of Israel, in the west coastlands.»
24.16: From the ends of the earth we hear songs: "Glory to the righteous." But I said, "I am lost. I am lost, woe is me." The plunderers plunder, the plunderers plunder without measure.
24.17: Terror, a pit, and a snare are upon you, inhabitant of the earth.
24.18: He who flees from the cry of terror will fall into the pit, and he who comes up from the midst of the pit will be caught in the net. For the floodgates above are opened, and the foundations of the earth are shaken.
24.19: The earth breaks violently, the earth bursts with a crash, the earth shakes forcefully.
24.20: The earth staggers like a drunken man, it sways like a hammock, its iniquity weighs upon it, it collapses never to rise again.
24.21: On that day, the Lord will visit the host on high and the kings of the earth on the earth.
24.22: And they will be gathered captive in the abyss and imprisoned in the dungeon; after many days they will be visited.
24.23: And the moon will turn red and the sun pale, because the Lord of hosts will reign on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem, and glory will be displayed before his elders.

25.1: Lord, you are my God, I will exalt you, I will praise your name, for you have done wonderful things, your plans formed long ago are faithful and firm.
25.2: For you have made the city a heap of stones and the fortified city a ruin; the citadel of the barbarians is no longer a city, it will never be rebuilt.
25.3: Therefore a mighty people will glorify you, the city of terrible nations will revere you.
25.4: You have been a fortress for the weak, a fortress for the poor in their distress, a shelter from the storm, a shade from the heat of the sun. For the breath of the tyrants is like a hurricane that beats against a wall.
25.5: Like the sun's heat on arid land, you cut down the insolence of barbarians; like the sun's heat, the oppressors' song of triumph is stifled by the shadow of a cloud.
25.6: And the Lord of hosts will prepare for all the peoples on this mountain a feast of rich meats, a feast of fine wines, of meats full of marrow, of good and clarified wines.
25.7: And he will tear down on this mountain the veil that veiled all peoples and the covering that covered all nations,
25.8: He will destroy death forever. The Lord God will wipe away the tears from all faces; he will remove the reproach of his people from all the earth, for the Lord has spoken.
25.9: On that day it will be said: "This is our God, in whom we have hoped for salvation; this is the Lord, in whom we have hoped; let us rejoice and be glad in his salvation.".
25.10: For the hand of the Lord will rest on this mountain, but Moab will be trampled down on the spot, as straw is trampled down in the dung heap.
25.11: In this mire, he will stretch out his hands, as a swimmer stretches them out to swim, but the Lord will humble his pride, despite all the effort of his hands.,
25.12: "He will bring down your high walls, he will overthrow them, he will throw them to the ground, into the dust."»

26.1: On that day, this song will be sung in the land of Judah: We have a strong city. He makes his salvation its wall and its fortress.,
26.2: Open the gates, let in the just nation, which upholds the truth.
26.3: With a steady heart you ensure peace, peace, because he trusts in you.
26.4: Trust in the Lord forever, for the Lord is the rock of ages.
26.5: He humbled those who lived on the heights, he brought down the proud city, he brought it down to the ground and made it touch the dust.
26.6: It is trampled underfoot, beneath the feet of the humble, the steps of the unfortunate.
26.7: The path of the righteous is level, and straight is the way that you make straight.
26.8: Yes, on the path of your judgments, we awaited you, O Lord; your name and your memory were the desire of our souls.
26.9: My soul longed for you during the night and within me my spirit sought you, for when your judgments are exercised on the earth, the inhabitants of the world learn justice.
26.10: If grace is shown to the wicked, they do not learn righteousness; in the land of uprightness, they act perversely and do not see the majesty of the Lord.
26.11: Lord, your hand is raised, but they do not see it. They will see your zeal for your people and be ashamed; the fire made for your adversaries will consume them.
26.12: Lord, you will grant us peace, for all our work, you have done it for us.
26.13: Lord, our God, other masters besides you have ruled over us, but through you alone we can celebrate your name.
26.14: The dead will live no more, the shades will not rise again. You have visited them and exterminated them, and you have annihilated all memory of them.
26.15: You have increased the nation, Lord, you have increased the nation and manifested your glory, you have extended the borders of the land.
26.16: Lord, in their distress they sought you; they cried out in anguish when you struck them.
26.17: Like a pregnant woman, about to give birth, writhing and crying out in her pains, so we were before your face, Lord.
26.18: We conceived in pain and gave birth to wind, we did not give salvation to the earth and no inhabitants of the world were born.
26.19: Your dead will live, my corpses will rise again, wake up and sing, you who lie in the dust, for your dew, Lord, is a dew of light and the earth will give back to the day the departed.
26.20: Go, my people, enter your chambers and shut your doors behind you, hide yourselves for a little while, until the wrath has passed.
26.21: For behold, the Lord is coming out of his dwelling place, to visit the iniquity of the inhabitants of the earth, and the earth will uncover the blood it has drunk and will no longer hide its slain.

27.1: On that day the Lord will visit with his hard, great and strong sword Leviathan, the swift serpent, Leviathan, the twisting serpent, and he will slay the monster that is in the sea.
27.2: On that day we will say: "A vine with generous wine, sing its praises.
27.3: I, the Lord, am the one who guards it; I water it at all times, lest anyone enter it; night and day I guard it; I am no longer angry.
27.4: Who will give me thorns and briers to fight against? I will march against them, I will burn them all.
27.5: Or, let them cling to my protection, let them make peace with me, let them make peace with me.
27.6: In the days to come, Jacob will take root, Israel will blossom and sprout, and the face of the world will be filled with its fruits.
27.7: Did he strike him as he struck those who struck him? Did he kill him as those he had killed were killed?
27.8: With restraint, you punished him by exile. He drove him out with an imperious breath, on a day of eastern wind.
27.9: Thus therefore the iniquity of Jacob will be atoned for, and this is all the fruit of the forgiveness of his sin: When he has ground the altar stones to powder, like limestone, the sacred poles and the images of the Sun will not stand again.
27.10: For the fortified city has become a solitude, a desolate and abandoned dwelling, like the desert, where calves graze, lie down and browse on branches.
27.11: When the branches are dry, they are broken off, and women come and burn them. For this is not a wise people, therefore their Maker will have no pity on them, and their Creator will show them no mercy.
27.12: And it will come to pass on that day, that the Lord will thresh the grain from the course of the river to the Wadi of Egypt, and you will be gathered one by one, children of Israel.
27.13: And it will come to pass, on that day, that the great trumpet will sound, and those who were lost in the land of Assyria and those who were banished in the land of Egypt will return and worship before the Lord on the holy mountain in Jerusalem.

28.1: Woe to the proud diadem of the drinkers of Ephraim, to the fleeting flower that makes the brilliance of their adornment, on the peaks of the fertile valley of men drunk with wine.
28.2: Behold, a strong and mighty enemy is coming from the Lord, like a hailstorm, a destructive hurricane, like a flood of overflowing waters, he will throw him down with violence.
28.3: The proud diadem of the drinkers of Ephraim will be trampled underfoot,
28.4: And it will be with the ephemeral flower that makes the brilliance of their adornment, at the top of the fertile valley, as with a fig ripened before summer, he who sees it, barely has it in his hand, before he swallows it.
28.5: On that day, the Lord will be a shining diadem and a crown of glory for the remnant of his people,
28.6: a spirit of justice for him who sits for justice, a strength for those who repel the assault at the gate.
28.7: They too are troubled by wine, led astray by strong drink; priest and prophet are led astray by strong drink, they are overwhelmed by wine, led astray by strong drink, they are troubled when prophesying, they reel when judging,
28.8: All the tables are covered in disgusting vomit, there is no more room.
28.9: «"To whom does he want to teach wisdom and to whom does he want to make the lesson understood? To children barely weaned, barely detached from their mother's breast?"
28.10: Because it's order upon order, order upon order, rule upon rule, rule upon rule, sometimes this, sometimes that.»
28.11: Well, it is through people who stutter and in a foreign tongue that the Lord will speak to this people.
28.12: He had said, "This is the place of rest; let the weary rest, and here is the place of relief," but they would not listen.
28.13: The word of the Lord will therefore be for them command upon command, command upon command, rule upon rule, rule upon rule, sometimes this, sometimes that, so that they may go and fall backward, be broken, be caught in the net.
28.14: Therefore, listen to the word of the Lord, you mockers, you leaders of this people who are in Jerusalem.
28.15: You say: «We have made a pact with death, we have made an agreement with Sheol. The overwhelming scourge will pass through and not reach us, for we have made lies our refuge and fraud our shelter.»
28.16: Therefore this is what the Lord God says: “See, I lay a stone in Zion, a tested stone, a precious cornerstone, a sure foundation: whoever trusts in it will never be shaken away.”.
28.17: I will take right as the rule and justice as the standard. And hail will sweep away the refuge of lies, and the waters will carry away your shelter.
28.18: Your pact with death will be shattered and your agreement with Sheol will not stand; when the overwhelming scourge passes, it will crush you.,
28.19: As often as it passes, it will seize you. For it will pass tomorrow and tomorrow, day and night, terror alone will teach you a lesson.
28.20: Because "the bed is too short to lie on and the blanket too narrow to wrap oneself in"«
28.21: for the Lord will rise up as at the mountain of Pharasim, he will tremble with indignation as in the valley of Gibeon, to accomplish his work, a singular work, to execute his task, a strange task.
28.22: And now stop mocking, lest your bonds be tightened, for a destruction is decreed, I have heard from the Lord of hosts against all the earth.
28.23: Listen closely and hear my voice; pay attention and hear my words.
28.24: Does the plowman, in order to sow, always have to plow, open up and harrow his land?
28.25: Won't he, when he has leveled the surface, scatter the nigella, sow the cumin, put the wheat in rows, the barley in its place and the spelt along the edge?
28.26: It is his God who teaches him these rules and instructs him.
28.27: Because it is not with a sledge that one treads the nigella and the wheel of the cart does not pass over the cumin, but one beats the nigella with the stick and the cumin with the wand.
28.28: The wheat is trodden, but care is taken not to beat it all the time; the cart wheel and the horses are pushed over it, but without crushing it.
28.29: This too comes from the Lord of hosts; he is admirable in his counsels and rich in his resources.

29.1: Woe to Ariel, to Ariel, to the city where David pitched his tent. Add year upon year, as the solemnities run their cycle,
29.2: And I will hold Ariel close, and there will be nothing but complaints and moans. But she will be to me like Ariel.
29.3: I will encamp all around you, I will surround you with armed posts and I will establish entrenchments against you.
29.4: You will be humbled, your voice will rise from the earth and your muffled accents will be heard from the dust, your voice will come out of the earth like that of a ghost and your speech will rise from the dust like a whisper.
29.5: And the multitude of your enemies will be like fine dust, and the multitude of warriors like chaff that blows away.
29.6: And it will come to pass that suddenly, in an instant, you will be visited by the Lord of hosts with a crash, thunder and great noise, whirlwind, tempest and devouring flame of fire.
29.7: And it will be like a dream, a vision of the night, of the multitude of all the nations that will fight against Ariel and of all those who will fight against her and her fortress and besiege her closely.
29.8: As a hungry man dreams that he eats and, upon waking, his soul is empty, and as a thirsty man dreams that he drinks and, upon waking, is exhausted and always thirsty, so it will be with the multitude of all the nations, who march against Mount Zion.
29.9: Be astonished and amazed. Be blinded and blinded. They are drunk, but not on wine; they stagger, but not on strong liquors.
29.10: For the Lord has poured out upon you a spirit of lethargy; he has closed your eyes, the prophets, and he has put a veil over your heads, the seers.
29.11: And every vision has become to you like the words of a sealed book. It is handed to a man who can read, saying, «Read this,» and he says, «I cannot, for this book is sealed.»
29.12: It is presented to a man who cannot read, and he is told, "Read this," and he says, "I cannot read."«
29.13: The Lord said: Because this people draws near with words and honors me with their lips, while their hearts are far from me, and their worship is based on a commandment learned from men,
29.14: Because of this, I will continue to perform wonders with this people, strange wonders. And the wisdom of their wise men will perish, and the understanding of their teachers will be darkened.
29.15: Woe to those who hide their plans from the Lord, whose work is done in darkness, and who say, «Who sees us? Who knows us?»
29.16: What madness! Will the potter then be valued as if he were clay, so that the work says of the craftsman, "He did not make me," and the vessel says of the potter, "He knows nothing about it?"«
29.17: Won't Lebanon soon be transformed into an orchard, and won't the orchard be considered a forest?
29.18: On that day the deaf will hear the words of the book, and out of darkness and gloom the blind will see.
29.19: The humble will rejoice more and more in the Lord, and the poorest will exult in the Holy One of Israel.
29.20: For the oppressor will have disappeared and the mocker will have perished, and all who plot iniquity will be exterminated.,
29.21: those who condemn a man for a word, who lay traps for the one who confounds them at the gate, and who destroy the righteous with their lies.
29.22: Therefore this is what the Lord says to the house of Jacob, he who redeemed Abraham: Jacob will no longer be put to shame, and his brow will no longer grow pale.
29.23: For when he and his children see the work of my hands in their midst, they will sanctify my name, they will sanctify the Holy One of Jacob, and they will revere the God of Israel.
29.24: Those who were foolish in mind will learn wisdom, and those who murmured will receive instruction.

30.1: Woe to the rebellious children, declares the Lord, who make plans, but not me, who enter into agreements, but not my spirit, heaping sin upon sin.
30.2: They go down the road to Egypt, without having consulted my mouth, to take refuge under the protection of Pharaoh and to shelter in the shade of Egypt.
30.3: The protection of Pharaoh will be a shame for you, and the refuge in the shadow of Egypt, a confusion.
30.4: The princes of Judah are already at Tanis, and his envoys have advanced as far as Hanes.,
30.5: They are all confused because of a people who are of no use to them, who give them neither help nor assistance, but only confusion and ignominy.
30.6: Oracle of the beasts of the Negev: Through a land of distress and anguish, from which come the lion and the lioness, the viper and the flying dragon, they carry their riches on the backs of donkeys and their treasures on the humps of camels, to a people who are of no use.
30.7: Egypt's help will be vanity and nothingness; therefore I call her: "The proud one who sits still."«
30.8: Now go, engrave this on a tablet in their presence and write it in a book, so that it may be a testimony for all time.
30.9: For they are a rebellious people, they are faithless children, children who refuse to listen to the law of the Lord.
30.10: They say to the seers, "Do not see," and to the prophets, "Do not prophesy to us the truth; tell us pleasant things, prophesy illusions.".
30.11: "Get out of the way, turn aside from the path, remove the Holy One of Israel from before our eyes."»
30.12: Therefore, this is what the Holy One of Israel says: Because you despise this word and trust in violence and deceit, and rely on them,
30.13: Because of this, this iniquity will be to you like a crack that threatens to ruin and juts out on a high wall, which suddenly, in an instant, collapses.
30.14: It breaks like a clay pot, which is smashed without pity, without finding in its fragments a piece to take fire from the brazier, or to draw water from the cistern.
30.15: For thus says the Lord God, the Holy One of Israel: Through repentance and quiet expectation you would be saved; in rest and trust would be your strength. But you were unwilling.
30.16: And you said, "No, but we will flee on horses." Well, you will flee. "We will fly on steeds." Well, those who pursue you will be faster.
30.17: A thousand at the threat of one and at the threat of five you will flee, until you are but a remnant, like a mast on the mountaintop, like a signal on the hill.
30.18: Therefore the Lord waits to be gracious to you; therefore he will rise up to show you mercy, for the Lord is a just God. Blessed are all who hope in him.
30.19: For, O people who dwell in Zion, in Jerusalem, you shall weep no more. At your first cry, he will be gracious to you; as soon as he hears you, he will answer you.
30.20: The Lord will give you the bread of anguish and the water of distress, and your teachers will no longer hide themselves, and your eyes will see your teachers.,
30.21: and your ears will hear behind you a voice saying, «This is the way, follow it,» when you turn to the right or to the left.
30.22: You shall treat as unclean the silver that covers your idols and the gold that adorns your statues, you shall reject them as something unclean: «Get out of here!» you shall tell them.
30.23: The Lord will send showers upon the seed you have sown in the earth, and the bread that the earth yields will be rich and plentiful; your flocks in that day will graze in broad pastures.,
30.24: and the oxen and donkeys, which work the land, will eat tasty fodder winnowed with the shovel and the winnowing fan.
30.25: And on every high mountain and on every high hill there will be streams, rivers of water, on the day of the great slaughter when the towers fall.
30.26: The light of the moon will be like the light of the sun, and the light of the sun will be seven times greater, like the light of seven days, on the day when the Lord binds up the wound of his people and heals the sores with which he struck them.
30.27: Behold, the name of the Lord comes from afar, his anger burns and his ardor is overwhelming, his lips breathe fury and his tongue is like a devouring fire.
30.28: His breath is like an overflowing torrent, rising up to the neck, to sift the nations with the sieve of destruction and to put a bridle of straying in the jaws of the peoples.
30.29: Then you will sing songs as on the night when a feast is celebrated, and your heart will be joyful, like one who goes up to the sound of the flute, to go to the mountain of the Lord, to the rock of Israel.
