30.30: The Lord will make his majestic voice resound and will show his bowing arm, in the heat of his anger and the flame of a devouring fire, in the storm, the downpour, and the hailstones.
30.31: And Assyria will tremble at the voice of the Lord; he will strike with his staff,
30.32: and, each time the fatal staff passes over him, which the Lord will lower upon him, the tambourines and harps will be sounded and he will fight against it with redoubled blows.
30.33: For Topheth has long been prepared, he too is ready for the king, the Lord has made him wide and deep, there is on his pyre fire and wood in abundance, the breath of the Lord, like a torrent of sulfur, sets him ablaze.

31.1: Woe to those who go down to Egypt for help, who rely on horses, who trust in chariots because they are many, and in horsemen because they are strong, but do not look to the Holy One of Israel or seek the Lord.
31.2: However, he is wise; he brings disaster; he does not revoke his words; he will rise up against the house of the wicked and against the help of those who commit iniquity.
31.3: The Egyptian is a man and not a God, his horses are flesh and not spirit; the Lord will stretch out his hand, the one who helps will stumble and the one helped will fall, and they will all perish together.
31.4: For thus the Lord spoke to me: as the lion roars, as the young lion, over its prey, when the multitude of shepherds gathers against it, and is not frightened by their cries, nor disturbed by their numbers: so the Lord of hosts will come down to fight, on Mount Zion and on its hill.
31.5: Like birds spreading their wings over their nest, so the Lord of hosts will cover Jerusalem; he will cover, he will save, he will pass through, he will deliver.
31.6: Return, then, to the one from whom you have strayed so far, children of Israel.
31.7: For on that day everyone will reject their silver idols and their gold idols, which you made with your own hands to sin.
31.8: And Assyria will fall by a sword not of man, a sword not of mortal will devour him; he will flee before the sword, and his young men will be subject to tribute.
31.9: His rock will flee in terror and his trembling princes will desert the banner, oracle of the Lord, who has his fire in Zion and his furnace in Jerusalem.

32.1: Behold, a king shall reign according to justice, and princes shall rule with righteousness.
32.2: And each of them will be a shelter from the wind and a refuge from the storm, like streams of water in a dry land, like the shade of a great rock in a desolate country.
32.3: The eyes of those who see will no longer be blinded, and the ears of those who hear will be attentive.
32.4: The hearts of frivolous men will be adept at understanding, and the tongues of stutterers at speaking clearly.
32.5: The fool will no longer be called noble, and the deceitful will no longer be called magnanimous.
32.6: For the fool speaks foolishly, and his heart is set on iniquity, to do ungodly things and to utter false words against the Lord, to leave the soul of the hungry hungry hungry and to deprive the thirsty of drink.
32.7: The weapons of the deceitful are disloyal; he plots schemes to destroy the humble with lies and the poor even as he speaks of justice.
32.8: But the nobleman has noble designs and he will rise up for noble deeds.
32.9: Carefree women, rise up, listen to my voice. Carefree girls, lend an ear to my words.
32.10: In a year and a few days you will tremble, carefree, because there will be no grape harvest, the fruit harvest will not take place.
32.11: Be filled with dread, you nonchalant ones. Tremble, you careless ones. Take off your rich clothes, strip yourselves bare, gird your loins with sacks.
32.12: We lament, beating our breasts, over the beautiful countryside, over the fruitful vineyards.
32.13: On the land of my people grow thorns and briers, even on all the pleasure houses of the joyous city.
32.14: For the palace is abandoned, the noisy city has become a solitude, Ophel and the Watchtower serve forever as dens, places of frolicking for wild asses and pasture for flocks.
32.15: Until a spirit from on high is poured out upon us, and the desert becomes an orchard, and the orchard is called a forest.
32.16: Then righteousness will dwell in the desert, and justice will be established in the orchard.
32.17: And the product of justice will be peace, and the fruit of justice will be rest and security forever.
32.18: My people will dwell in a peaceful abode, in secure dwellings, in tranquil homes.
32.19: But the forest will have fallen under the hail and the city deeply sunk.
32.20: Blessed are you who sow everywhere near the waters and who let the ox and the donkey roam free.

33.1: Woe to you, destroyer who is not destroyed, plunderer who has not yet been plundered. When you have finished destroying, you will be destroyed; when you have finished plundering, you will be plundered.
33.2: Lord, have mercy on us. It is in you that we hope; be their arm each morning and our deliverance in times of distress.
33.3: At the sound of your thunder, the peoples flee; when you rise up, the nations scatter.
33.4: Your loot is gathered like a grasshopper gathers, it is rushed upon like a swarm of grasshoppers.
33.5: The Lord is exalted because he dwells on high; he fills Zion with equity and justice.
33.6: Your days are secure, and you will have abundant salvation, wisdom and knowledge; the fear of the Lord is your treasure.
33.7: Now their heroes are shouting in the streets, and the messengers of peace are weeping bitterly.
33.8: The roads are deserted, there are no more passers-by on the paths. He has broken the treaty and scorned the cities; he does not respect men.
33.9: The country mourns and languishes, Lebanon is confused and withered. Sharon has become like the Arabah, Bashan and Carmel shake off their leaves.
33.10: Now I will arise, says the Lord, now I will stand up, now I will be exalted.
33.11: You have conceived straw and you will give birth to stubble; your breath is the fire that will consume you.
33.12: And the peoples will be like lime kilns, like cut thorns that burn in the fire.
33.13: You who are far away, listen to what I have done, and you who are near, know my power.
33.14: The sinners in Zion trembled, and terror seized the wicked: «Who among us will dwell in the consuming fire? Who among us will dwell in the eternal flames?»
33.15: He who walks in righteousness and speaks uprightly, who rejects extorted gains, who shakes his hands so as not to accept a bribe, who closes his ears to bloodshed and veils his eyes so as not to see evil:
33.16: He will dwell in high places, the fortress built on the rock will be his refuge, his bread will be given him and his waters will never run dry.
33.17: Your eyes will behold the king in his beauty, they will see an open land in the distance.
33.18: Your heart will remember its terrors: "Where is the scribe? Where is the tax collector who held the scales? Where is the officer who counted the laps?"«
33.19: The insolent people, you will no longer see them, the people with obscure and incomprehensible language, who stammer an unintelligible tongue.
33.20: Look upon Zion, the city of our feasts, let your eyes see Jerusalem, a happy dwelling place, a tent that will not be moved, whose stakes will never be pulled out, and whose cords will never be taken away.
33.21: There truly the Lord in his majesty dwells for us; there are rivers and broad canals, where no rowboat will venture, where no mighty ship will ever enter.
33.22: For the Lord is our judge, the Lord is our lawgiver, the Lord is our king, he is the one who will save us.
33.23: Your rigging is loose; it no longer holds the mast to its base or supports the unfurled flag. So the spoils of a rich haul are divided, even the lame take part in the pillaging.
33.24: No inhabitant says, "I am sick." The people who dwell in Zion have received forgiveness for their iniquity.

34.1: Come near, you nations, to hear; listen, you peoples. Let the earth hear, and all that is in it, the world and all that it produces.
34.2: For the Lord is angry with all nations and furious with all their armies; he has devoted them to slaughter.
34.3: Their dead will be thrown away without burial, their corpses will exhale infection, and the mountains will melt in their blood.
34.4: All the host of heaven shall be reduced to dust, and the heavens shall be rolled up like a scroll, and all their host shall fall, as the leaf of the vine falls, as the leaf of the fig tree falls.
34.5: «For my sword has drunk its fill in the heavens, and behold, it descends upon Edom, upon the people whom I have devoted to destruction, to judge them.”.
34.6: The Lord's sword is filled with blood, dripping with fat, with the blood of lambs and goats, with the fat of rams' kidneys. For the Lord offers a sacrifice at Bozrah and a great slaughter in the land of Edom.
34.7: With them fall the buffaloes and the oxen with the bulls. Their land is drunk with blood and their dust runs with fat.
34.8: For it is a day of vengeance for the Lord, a year of revenge for the cause of Zion.
34.9: Its streams will be turned into pitch, its dust into sulfur, and its land will become burning pitch.,
34.10: which will not be extinguished by night or day, whose smoke will rise eternally. From age to age it will remain desolate, forever no one will pass through it.
34.11: The pelican and the hedgehog will possess it, the owl and the raven will dwell there. And he will spread over it the line of chaos and the level of the void.
34.12: There will no longer be any nobles to proclaim a king, and all his princes will be annihilated.
34.13: Thorns will grow in its palaces, brambles and thistles in its fortresses. It will be a den of jackals and a park for ostriches.
34.14: Wild cats and dogs will meet there, and satyrs will call to one another. There too, the specter of the night will make its home and find its resting place.
34.15: There the snake will make its nest and lay its eggs, incubate them, and gather its young under its shade. There too, all the vultures will gather.
34.16: Search in the book of the Lord and read: Not one of them is missing, not one is lacking. For it is the mouth of the Lord that commanded it and his breath that gathered them together.
34.17: It is he who cast the lot for them and his hand that divided the land between them by a ruler; they shall possess it forever, from generation to generation they shall dwell in it.

35.1: The desert and the arid land will rejoice, the steppe will be filled with gladness and will blossom like the narcissus,
35.2: It will blossom and rejoice, it will shout for joy. The glory of Lebanon will be given to it, with the majesty of Carmel and Sharon. They will see the glory of the Lord, the majesty of our God.
35.3: Strengthen weak hands and fortify shaky knees.
35.4: Tell those whose hearts are troubled: «Take courage, do not fear, here is your God, vengeance is coming, divine retribution, he himself is coming and will save you.»
35.5: Then the eyes of the blind will be opened, then the ears of the deaf will be opened.
35.6: The lame will leap like a deer, and the tongue of the mute will shout for joy. For waters will burst forth in the desert and streams in the steppe.
35.7: The scorched ground will turn into a lake and the weathered earth into springs of water; the den where the jackals lay will become a park of reeds and rushes.
35.8: There will be a road there, a way, which will be called the holy way; no impure person will pass on it; it is only for them; those who follow it, the simple ones themselves, will not go astray.
35.9: There will be no lion there, no ferocious beast will set foot there, nor will it be found there. The liberated will walk there,
35.10: And the redeemed of the Lord will return, they will come to Zion with shouts of joy, eternal gladness will crown their heads, joy and gladness will fill them, sorrow and sighing will flee away.

36.1: In the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah, Sennacherib king of Assyria came up against all the fortified cities of Judah and captured them.
36.2: And the king of Assyria sent from Lachish to Jerusalem, to King Hezekiah, his chief cupbearer with a large force; the chief cupbearer stopped at the aqueduct of the upper pool, on the way to the Fuller's Field.
36.3: Eliacim, son of Helkiah, head of the king's household, went to him, with Shebna the secretary and Joah, son of Asaph, the recorder.
36.4: The chief cupbearer said to them, «Tell Hezekiah: This is what the great king, the king of Assyria, says: What is this confidence you are relying on? I said: This advice and this strength for war are empty words.
36.5: And now, who do you trust to rebel against me?
36.6: Behold, you rely on the support of this broken reed, Egypt which penetrates and pierces the hand of whoever leans on it: such is Pharaoh, king of Egypt, to all who trust in him.
36.7: Perhaps you will say to me: We trust in the Lord our God. But is this not the one whose high places and altars Hezekiah removed, saying to Judah and Jerusalem: You shall worship before this altar?
36.8: Now, make a deal with my master, the king of Assyria: I will give you two thousand horses, if you can provide riders to ride them.
36.9: How could you repel even one of the least of my master's servants? Therefore you put your trust in Egypt for chariots and horses.
36.10: Now, was it against the Lord's will that I went up against this land to destroy it? The Lord said to me, "Go up against this land and destroy it."»
36.11: Eliakim, Shebna, and Joah said to the chief cupbearer, «Speak to your servants in Aramaic, for we understand it, and do not speak to us in the Hebrew language in the hearing of the people who are on the wall.»
36.12: The chief cupbearer replied, "Did my master send me to speak these words to you and your master? Was it not to these men sitting on the wall, to eat their excrement and drink their urine with you?"«
36.13: Then the chief cupbearer stepped forward and shouted aloud in the Jewish language, saying: «Listen to the words of the great king, the king of Assyria:
36.14: Thus said the king: Do not let Hezekiah deceive you, for he will not be able to deliver you.
36.15: and do not let Hezekiah persuade you to trust in the Lord, saying, "The Lord will surely deliver us; this city will not be given into the hands of the king of Assyria.".
36.16: Do not listen to Hezekiah, for thus says the king of Assyria: Make peace with me and surrender to me, and let each of you eat from his own vine and each of you from his own fig tree, and let each of you drink water from his own well,
36.17: until I come and take you to a land like your own, a land of wheat and wine, a land of bread and vineyards.
36.18: Do not let Hezekiah deceive you by saying, "The Lord will deliver us." Have the gods of the nations each delivered their own land from the hand of the king of Assyria?
36.19: Where are the gods of Emath and Arphad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim? Have they delivered Samaria from my hand?
36.20: Which of the gods of all these lands have delivered their lands from my hand, so that the Lord may deliver Jerusalem from my hand?»
36.21: They remained silent and did not answer a word, for the king had given this order: "You shall not answer him."«
36.22: And Eliacim, son of Helkiah, head of the king's household, Shebna the secretary, and Joah, son of Asaph, the recorder, came to Hezekiah with their clothes torn and reported to him the words of the chief cupbearer.

37.1: When King Hezekiah heard this report, he tore his clothes, covered himself with sackcloth, and went into the house of the Lord.
37.2: He sent Eliacim, the head of his household, Shebna, the secretary, and the elders of the priests, covered in sackcloth, to Isaiah, the son of Amos, the prophet.
37.3: They told him, "This is what Hezekiah said: This day is a day of distress, punishment, and disgrace, because the children are about to come out of the womb, and there is no strength to give birth.
37.4: Perhaps the Lord your God will hear the words of the chief cupbearer, whom the king of Assyria, his master, has sent to insult the living God, and will punish him for the words the Lord your God has heard. Therefore, pray for the remnant that is left.
37.5: King Hezekiah's servants went to Isaiah,
37.6: And Isaiah said to them, «This is what you are to say to your master: Thus says the Lord: Do not be afraid of the words that you have heard, by which the servants of the king of Assyria have insulted me.
37.7: »I am putting a spirit in him so that, upon hearing a rumor, he will return to his own country, and I will cause him to fall by the sword in his own country.”
37.8: The chief cupbearer returned and found the king of Assyria attacking Lobna, for he had learned that his master had left Lachis.
37.9: Now the king of Assyria received a report concerning Tharaca, king of Ethiopia, who was told, «He has set out to wage war against you.» Hearing this, he sent messengers to Hezekiah, saying:
37.10: «Say this to Hezekiah, king of Judah: Do not let your God, in whom you trust, deceive you by saying, ‘Jerusalem will not be given into the hands of the king of Assyria.’”.
37.11: Behold, you have heard what the kings of Assyria did to all the countries, subjecting them to anathema. And you would be delivered.
37.12: Did their gods deliver them, those nations that my fathers destroyed: Gozan, Haram, Reseph, and the sons of Eden, who were in Telasar?
37.13: Where are the king of Emath, the king of Arphad, and the king of the city of Sepharvaim, of Anah, and of Ava?»
37.14: Hezekiah, having received the letter from the messengers, read it, then Hezekiah went up to the house of the Lord and spread it out before the Lord.
37.15: And Hezekiah prayed before the Lord, saying:
37.16: «"Lord of hosts, God of Israel, seated upon the Cherubim, you alone are God over all the kingdoms of the earth, you who made the heavens and the earth.
37.17: Lord, incline your ear and hear. Lord, open your eyes and see. Hear all the words of Sennacherib, who has sent insults against the living God.
37.18: It is true, Lord, that the kings of Assyria destroyed all the nations and devastated their territories,
37.19: and that they threw their gods into the fire, for they were not gods, but works of human hands, wood and stone, and they destroyed them.
37.20: Now, Lord our God, save us from the hand of Sennacherib, and let all the kingdoms of the earth know that you alone are the Lord.»
37.21: And Isaiah, son of Amos, sent word to Hezekiah: «Thus says the Lord, God of Israel: The prayer that you have addressed to me concerning Sennacherib, king of Assyria, I have heard.
37.22: This is the word that the Lord has spoken against him: She despises you, she mocks you, the virgin daughter of Zion; she shakes her head behind you, the daughter of Jerusalem.
37.23: Whom have you insulted and outraged? Against whom have you raised your voice and lifted your eyes? Against the Holy One of Israel.
37.24: Through your servants you have insulted the Lord and you have said: With my many chariots I have ascended the mountain peaks, the farthest reaches of Lebanon, I will cut down its tallest cedars, its finest cypresses, and I will reach its highest summit and its forest like an orchard.
37.25: I dug and got water to drink; with the soles of my feet I will dry up all the rivers of Egypt.
37.26: Have you not heard that I did these things long ago and that I formed them from ancient times? Now I am bringing them to pass, so that you may reduce the fortified cities to heaps of ruins.
37.27: Their inhabitants are powerless, in terror and confusion; they are like the grass of the fields and the tender greenery like the turf of the roofs, like wheat that dries up before it is ripe.
37.28: But I know when you sit down, when you go out and when you come in, I know your fury against me.
37.29: Because you are furious with me and your arrogance has reached my ears, I will put my ring in your nostril and my bit in your lips and I will make you return by the way you came.
37.30: And this will be a sign for you: This year you will eat what grows of itself, in the second year you will eat what springs from itself, but in the third year you will sow and reap, plant vineyards and eat their fruit.
37.31: What has been saved from the house of Judah, what remains, will still put forth roots underneath and bear fruit above.
37.32: For out of Jerusalem a remnant will go forth, and out of Mount Zion survivors. This is what the zeal of the Lord of hosts will accomplish.
37.33: Therefore, this is what the Lord says concerning the king of Assyria: He will not enter this city, nor will he shoot an arrow at it, nor will he present shields to it, nor will he build siege works against it.
37.34: He will return by the way he came and will not enter this city, declares the Lord.
37.35: I will protect this city to save it, for my own sake and for the sake of David, my servant.»
37.36: And the angel of the Lord went out and struck down
37.37: And Sennacherib, king of Assyria, having broken camp, departed and returned and remained in Nineveh.
37.38: As he was worshiping in the house of Nesroch, his god, Adramelech and Sarasar, his sons, struck him with the sword and fled to the land of Ararat. And Esarhaddon, his son, reigned in his place.

38.1: At that time, Hezekiah became ill and was at the point of death. The prophet Isaiah, son of Amos, came to him and said, «This is what the Lord says: Put your house in order, because you are going to die; you will not recover.»
38.2: Hezekiah turned his face to the wall and prayed to the Lord, saying:
38.3: «Remember, O Lord, that I have walked before you faithfully and with integrity, and have done what is good in your eyes.» And Hezekiah wept bitterly.
38.4: And the word of the Lord came to Isaiah in these words:
38.5: «Go and tell Hezekiah: «This is what the Lord, the God of your father David, says: I have heard your prayer and seen your tears; I will add fifteen years to your life.
38.6: I will deliver you and this city from the hand of the king of Assyria; I will protect this city.
38.7: And this is the sign to you from the Lord, by which you will know that the Lord will fulfill this word that he has spoken:
38.8: Now I will make the shadow go back the same distance it has gone down on the sundials of Ahaz, ten degrees. And the sun went back ten degrees on the distance it had gone down.
38.9: Written by Hezekiah, king of Judah, when he was sick and recovered from his illness:
38.10: I said: In the peace of my days I go to the gates of Sheol, I am deprived of the rest of my years.
38.11: I said: I will no longer see God, the Lord, in the land of the living; I will no longer see men among the inhabitants of the silent abode.
38.12: My home is taken away, swept from me like a shepherd's tent. Like a weaver, I was weaving my life, but he cuts me off from the loom. From day to night you will be done with me.
38.13: I remained silent until morning, like a lion, he broke all my bones, from day to night you will be finished with me.
38.14: Like the swallow, like the crane, I cry out, I moan like the dove, my eyes have grown weary of looking upwards: "Lord, I am being violated, be my guarantor."«
38.15: What can I say? He told me, he did it. I will walk humbly through all my years, remembering the bitterness in my soul.
38.16: Lord, this is life, in all this is the life of my spirit. You heal me, you give me life:
38.17: Behold, my deepest bitterness has turned to peace. You have rescued my soul from the pit of perdition; you have cast all my sins behind your back.
38.18: For Sheol does not celebrate you, death does not sing your praises, those who go down to the pit no longer hope in your faithfulness.
38.19: The living one, the living one, it is he who celebrates you, as I do today, father will make known to his children your faithfulness.
38.20: The Lord was quick to save me; we will make the strings of my harp resound, all the days of our lives, before the house of the Lord.
38.21: Isaiah said, «Let a lump of figs be brought and applied to the boil, and let the king be healed.» And Hezekiah said, «What will be the sign that I shall go up to the house of the Lord?»

39.1: At that time, Merodach-Baladan son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent a letter and gifts to Hezekiah, for he had heard that he had been sick and had recovered.
39.2: Hezekiah rejoiced at the arrival of the envoys and he showed them his treasury house, the silver and gold, the spices and precious oil, all his armory and everything that was in his treasuries; there was nothing that Hezekiah did not show them in his house and in all his domains.
39.3: But Isaiah the prophet came to King Hezekiah and said to him, «What did these people say, and where did they come from?» Hezekiah replied, «They came to me from a distant land, from Babylon.»
39.4: Isaiah said, «What did they see in your house?» And Hezekiah answered, «They saw everything in my house; there is nothing among my treasures that I did not show them.»
39.5: And Isaiah said to Hezekiah, «Hear the word of the Lord of hosts:
39.6: “The days will come when everything in your house and everything your ancestors have stored up until this day will be carried off to Babylon; nothing will be left,” says the Lord.
39.7: And some of your sons, who will come from you, whom you will have fathered, will be taken to become eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.»
39.8: Hezekiah answered Isaiah, «The word of the Lord that you have spoken is good.» And he added, «For there will be peace and stability in my lifetime.».

40.1: Comfort, comfort my people, says your God.
40.2: Speak tenderly to Jerusalem and cry out to her: That her servitude is over, that her iniquity is expiated, that she has received from the Lord's hand double for her sins.
40.3: A voice cries out: Prepare the way of the Lord in the wilderness, make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
40.4: Let every valley be raised up, and every mountain and hill made low; let the high become a plain and the steep rocks a valley.
40.5: Then the glory of the Lord will appear, and all flesh without exception will see it, for the mouth of the Lord has spoken.
40.6: A voice says, "Cry out," and the response is, "What shall I cry out?" "All flesh is like grass, and all its grace like the flower of the field:
40.7: The grass withers, the flower fades, when the breath of the Lord passes over it. Yes, man is like grass.
40.8: The grass withers, the flower fades, but the word of God endures forever.»
40.9: Go up on a high mountain, you who bring good news to Zion; lift up your voice with strength; you who bring good news to Jerusalem, lift it up, do not be afraid; say to the cities of Judah, «Here is your God.»
40.10: Behold, the Lord God comes with power, his arm exercises dominion. Behold, his reward is with him and his recompense is before him.
40.11: Like a shepherd, he will tend his flock, he will gather the lambs in his arms and carry them in his bosom, he will gently lead those that are nursing.
40.12: Who has measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, or estimated the expanse of the heavens with his outstretched fingers, or gauged all the dust of the earth with a bushel, or weighed the mountains in a scale and the hills in a balance?
40.13: Who directed the mind of the Lord, and who was his counselor to instruct him?
40.14: With whom did he consult, to enlighten him, to teach him the path of justice, to teach him wisdom and show him the way of understanding?
40.15: Behold, the nations are like a drop suspended from a bucket, they are considered like the dust in the balance, behold, the islands are the fine powder that blows away.
40.16: Lebanon is not enough for the fire and its animals are not enough for the holocaust.
40.17: All nations are as nothing before him; he considers them as nothing and vanity.
40.18: To whom then will you compare God, and what image will you prepare for him?
40.19: When the craftsman casts an idol, the goldsmith covers it with gold and melts silver chains onto it.
40.20: The poor man, who cannot offer much, chooses a wood that will not rot and goes to find a skilled craftsman to make an idol that will not waver.
40.21: Do you not know? Have you not heard? Have you not been told from the beginning? Have you not learned who laid the foundations of the earth?
40.22: He reigns above the vault of the earth and its inhabitants are like grasshoppers; he stretches out the heavens like a veil and spreads them out like a tent to dwell in.
40.23: He delivers the powerful to nothing and reduces the judges of the earth to nothing.
40.24: They are scarcely planted, scarcely sown, scarcely has their trunk taken root in the earth, when the wind blows on them and they wither and the hurricane carries them away like stubble.
40.25: "To whom then will you compare me, that I should be like him?" said the Saint.
40.26: Lift up your eyes on high and see: who created these things? He who brings out their army in order and calls them all by name; and because of the greatness of his power and the energy of his strength, not one is missing.
40.27: Why do you say, O Jacob, and speak thus, O Israel: «My way is hidden from the Lord, and my justice is disregarded by my God?»
40.28: Do you not know, have you not heard? The Lord is an eternal God, who created the ends of the earth, who does not tire or grow weary, and whose wisdom is unsearchable.
40.29: It gives strength to the weary and increases the vigor of the weak.
40.30: Young people grow tired and weary, and young people falter,
40.31: But those who trust in the Lord will renew their strength; they will soar on wings like eagles; they will run and not grow weary, they will walk and not be faint.

41.1: Islands, be silent to listen to me, and let the peoples renew their strength, let them approach, and then let them speak. Let us go together to judgment.
41.2: Who has raised up from the East him whose steps are met by justice? Who has given nations into his hand and subdued kings before him? He makes their swords fly to dust, he renders their bows like chaff before the wind.
41.3: He pursues them and passes peacefully, along a path his foot had never trod before.
41.4: Who did this? Who accomplished it? He who calls the generations from the beginning, I, the Lord, who am the first and who will also be with the last.
41.5: The islands see it and are seized with fear, the ends of the earth tremble, they approach and come.
41.6: Each one helps his companion and one says to the other: Take courage.
41.7: The blacksmith encourages the founder, the polisher with the hammer, the one who strikes on the anvil, saying of the welding: "It is good" then he fixes the god with nails, so that it does not move.
41.8: But you, Israel, my servant, Jacob, whom I have chosen, descendants of Abraham my friend,
41.9: You whom I took from the ends of the earth and called from its distant regions, you to whom I said, «You are my servant, I have chosen you and have not rejected you.»
41.10: Do not be afraid, for I am with you; do not be dismayed, for I am your God; I have taken hold of you and I help you and uphold you with my righteous right hand.
41.11: Behold, all who are inflamed against you shall be confounded and put to shame; they shall be like nothing, they shall perish, those who contend against you.
41.12: You will seek them but you will not find them, those who quarrel with you; they will be like nothing, reduced to nothing, those who wage war against you.
41.13: For I, the Lord your God, take you by the right hand, and say to you, «Do not be afraid, I am the one who comes to your aid.»
41.14: Do not be afraid, you worm Jacob, you few remnant of Israel, I will come to your aid, declares the Lord, and your Redeemer is the Holy One of Israel.
41.15: Behold, I will make you a sharp, new, double-edged sledge; you will trample the mountains and crush them, and make the hills like chaff.
41.16: You will winnow them, and the wind will carry them away, and the hurricane will scatter them. And you will rejoice greatly in the Lord, you will glory in the Holy One of Israel.
41.17: The unfortunate and the poor, who seek water and find none, and whose tongue is parched with thirst, I, the Lord, will answer them; I, the God of Israel, will not forsake them.
41.18: I will make rivers gush forth on the bare peaks and springs in the midst of the valleys; I will turn the desert into a pool and the parched land into fountains of water.
41.19: I will plant in the desert the cedar, the acacia, the myrtle, and the olive tree; I will plant in the steppe both the cypress, the plane tree, and the box tree,
41.20: So that they may see, know, observe and understand together, that the hand of the Lord has done these things and that the Holy One of Israel has created them.
41.21: Present your case, says the Lord, produce your reasons, says the King of Jacob.
41.22: Let them produce them and tell us what is to come. Tell us what the former things were, and we will apply our hearts to them, to know their outcome; or else let us hear of things to come.
41.23: Announce the things that will happen later and we will know that you are gods. Do good or do evil, let us see and admire together.
41.24: There you are, you are nothing and your work is nothing: abominable is he who chooses you.
41.25: I have stirred him up from the north and he comes, from the rising sun, he calls upon my name, he treads on the satraps as on mud, as the potter treads clay.
41.26: Who made it known from the beginning, so that we would have known it long beforehand, so that we could say, "Is it true?" No. No one announced it. No. No one made it heard. No. No one heard your words.
41.27: First I said to Zion, «Here they are,» and I am sending a messenger of good news to Jerusalem.
41.28: I look around and there is no one among them, not a single advisor, whom I can question and who will answer me.
41.29: Behold, they are all vanity, their works are worthless, their idols, a vain breath.

42.1: Here is my servant, whom I uphold, my chosen one, in whom my soul delights; I have put my Spirit on him, and he will spread justice among the nations.
42.2: He will not shout, he will not speak loudly, he will not make his voice heard in the streets.
42.3: He will not break the bruised reed, nor quench the smoldering wick. He will bring forth justice in truth,
42.4: He will not falter or be discouraged until he has established justice on earth, and the islands will wait for his law.
42.5: Thus says the Lord God, who created the heavens and stretched them out, who spread out the earth and its produce, who gives breath to the people who dwell on it and life to those who walk on it:
42.6: I, the Lord, have called you in righteousness and have taken you by the hand; I will keep you and make you a covenant for the people, a light for the nations,
42.7: to open the eyes of the blind, to bring captives out of prison, and those who sit in darkness out of the dungeon.
42.8: I am the Lord, that is my name, and I will not give my glory to another, nor my honor to idols,
42.9: The first things have already happened, and I announce new things; before they blossom, I let you hear them.
42.10: Sing to the Lord a new song, sing his praise from the ends of the earth, you who sail on the sea and who dwell in it, you islands and your inhabitants.
42.11: Let the desert and its cities raise their voices, the encampments inhabited by Cedar. Let the inhabitants of Sela exult with gladness, let them shout from the mountaintops.
42.12: Let them give glory to the Lord, let them proclaim his praise in the islands.
42.13: For the Lord goes forth like a hero, like a warrior, he stirs up his zeal, he raises the battle cry, a resounding cry, he displays his strength against his enemies.
42.14: For a long time I remained silent, I kept quiet, I restrained myself, like a woman giving birth, I moan, I sigh and I am panting.
42.15: I will devastate the mountains and hills and dry up all their greenery, I will turn the rivers into islands and I will dry up the lakes.
42.16: I will lead the blind by a way they do not know, I will guide them along paths they have not known; I will turn darkness into light before them and the rough places into level ground. These are the words I will fulfill; I will not fail.
42.17: They will be thrown back and put to shame, those who trust in idols, who say to molten images, "You are our gods."«
42.18: Deaf people, hear; blind people, open your eyes to see.
42.19: Who is blind but my servant, and deaf like my messenger whom I send? Who is blind like the one I befriended, blind like the servant of the Lord?
42.20: You saw many things and retained nothing; he had his ears open and heard nothing.
42.21: The Lord, because of his justice, has deigned to give a great and magnificent law.
42.22: And so this people is plundered and stripped bare. They have all been chained in caves, locked in dungeons, they are being pillaged and no one is freeing them, stripped bare and no one is saying, "Give back."«
42.23: Who among you will lend an ear to these things, pay attention, and listen from now on?
42.24: Who gave Jacob up to plunder and Israel to plunderers? Was it not the Lord, against whom we have sinned, whose ways they would not follow and whose law they would not obey?
42.25: He poured out upon them the fire of his wrath and the furies of war; it ignited all around him and he did not understand, it consumed him and he did not notice.

43.1: And now, this is what the Lord says—he who created you, O Jacob, he who formed you, O Israel: Do not be afraid, for I have redeemed you; I have called you by name; you are mine.
43.2: When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and through the rivers, they will not sweep you away; when you walk through the fire, you will not be burned; the flame will not set you ablaze.
43.3: For I, the Lord, am your God, the Holy One of Israel is your Savior. I have given Egypt as your ransom, Ethiopia and Seba in exchange for you.
43.4: Because you are precious in my eyes, honorable, and because I love you, I will give men in exchange for you and nations in exchange for your life.
43.5: Do not be afraid, for I am with you; from the east I will bring your descendants back, and from the west I will gather you.
43.6: I will say to the north, «Give them up,» and to the south, «Do not hold them back. Bring my sons from afar and my daughters from the ends of the earth,
43.7: all those who bear my name, whom I created for my glory, whom I formed and whom I made.
43.8: "Bring out the blind people who have eyes, and the deaf people who have ears."»
43.9: Let all the nations gather together and let the peoples assemble. Which of them has foretold these things and declared to us the ancient prophecies? Let them bring forth their witnesses and prove themselves, so that they may be heard and it may be said, «It is true.»
43.10: You are my witnesses, declares the Lord, and my servant whom I have chosen, so that you may acknowledge and believe me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed, nor will there be one after me.
43.11: I am the Lord, and there is no savior besides me.
43.12: It is I who announced, who saved, who foretold; it is not a foreign god among you, and you are my witnesses, declares the Lord. I am God.,
43.13: From now on I am also under his control, and there is no one who can deliver me from my hand; I will act, and who can stop it?
43.14: Thus says the Lord, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: Because of you, I have sent against Babylon and I am making them all go down as fugitives, the Chaldeans, on the ships of which they are so proud.
43.15: I am the Lord, your Holy One, the Creator of Israel, your king.
43.16: Thus says the Lord, who opened a way in the sea, a path in the mighty waters,
43.17: who put chariots and horses into the field, army and valiant warriors, all together they lay down never to rise again, they were smothered, they were extinguished like a wick.
43.18: Forget about past events and stop dwelling on things of the past.
43.19: Behold, I am about to do a marvelous new thing; it is about to spring forth, do you not perceive it? I will make a way in the wilderness, and rivers in the desert land.
43.20: The beasts of the field will glorify me, the jackals and the ostriches. For I will provide water in the desert and rivers in the desert, to give drink to my people, my chosen ones,
43.21: the people I have formed for myself, who will proclaim my praise.
43.22: However, you did not call upon me, O Jacob, nor did you concern yourself with me, O Israel.
43.23: You have not offered me the sheep of your burnt offerings, nor have you honored me with your sacrifices. I have not burdened you with offerings, nor have I wearied you with incense.
43.24: You did not buy me sweet cane with money, nor did you satisfy me with the fat of your victims, but you yourself have burdened me with your sins, you have wearied me with your iniquities.
43.25: It is I, it is I who erase your faults for love of me, and I will remember your sins no more.
43.26: Awaken my memories, let us plead together, speak for yourself to justify yourself.
43.27: Your first father sinned, and those who interpreted my law to you were faithless to me.
43.28: That is why I have degraded the princes of the sanctuary, I have given Jacob over to destruction and Israel to insults.

44.1: And now listen, Jacob, my servant, and you, Israel, whom I have chosen.
44.2: Thus says the Lord, who made you, who formed you from the womb and helped you: Do not be afraid, Jacob my servant, Israel, whom I have chosen.
44.3: For I will pour water on the thirsty land and streams on the dry ground. I will pour out my Spirit on your offspring and my blessing on your descendants.
44.4: And they will grow among the greenery, like willows by flowing streams.
44.5: One will say, "I belong to the Lord," another will call himself Jacob, and yet another will write on his hand, "The Lord's," and take the name Israel.
44.6: Thus says the Lord, the King of Israel and his Redeemer, the Lord of hosts: I am the first and I am the last and there is no God besides me.
44.7: Who is like me, let him speak, let him declare, let him show me, since I founded humanity in ancient times? Let them announce the future and what is to come.
44.8: Do not be afraid or discouraged. Have I not long ago told you and announced it to you? You are my witnesses. Is there a God besides me? There is no other Rock; I know of none.
44.9: The idol makers are all nothing but nothing and their masterpieces are useless; their witnesses, to their shame, see nothing and understand nothing.
44.10: Who created a god, who cast an idol, only to derive no benefit from it?
44.11: Behold, all his worshipers will be put to shame, and the workers are but men. Let them all assemble, let them come forward. They will tremble and be put to shame together.
44.12: The ironworker works with the chisel, he passes his work through the burning coals, he shapes it with the hammer, he works it with a strong arm. However, he is hungry and thus weak, he drinks no water and thus exhausted.
44.13: The woodworker stretches the string, he traces the shape with the pencil, shapes it with the chisel, measures it with the compass, he makes a figure of a man, the beautiful human figure, so that it may fit into a house.
44.14: A man goes to cut down cedars, he takes sessile oaks and oaks, he makes a choice among the trees of the forest, or he plants cedars and the rain makes them grow.
44.15: Man uses this wood for burning, he takes it to heat himself, he also lights it to bake his bread, he also makes a god of it and worships it, he makes an idol of it and he prostrates himself before it.
44.16: He burned half of it in the fire, and with the other half, he prepared his meat, cooked his roast, and ate his fill. He also warmed himself and said, "Ah, ah, I'm getting warm, I can feel the flame."»
44.17: From what remains, he makes his god, his idol, which he worships, prostrating himself before it, and praying, saying, "Deliver me, for you are my God."«
44.18: They do not know, they do not hear, because their eyes are covered so they cannot see and their hearts so they cannot understand.
44.19: And he doesn't look inward; he lacks the intelligence and common sense to say to himself: "I burned half of it in the fire, I also baked bread on the coals, I roasted meat and ate it, and with the rest I would commit an abomination, I would bow down before a tree trunk."«
44.20: He feeds on ashes; his deluded heart leads him astray. He will not save his soul, nor will he say, "Is not what is in my hand a lie?"«
44.21: Remember these things, O Jacob, O Israel, for you are my servant, I have formed you for my servant, O Israel, you will not be forgotten by me.
44.22: I have wiped away your transgressions like a cloud and your sins like a mist: return to me, for I have redeemed you.
44.23: Sing for joy, you heavens, for the Lord has done this. Resound, you depths of the earth. Burst into joy, you mountains and forests, with all your trees, for the Lord has redeemed Jacob and revealed his glory in Israel.
44.24: Thus says the Lord, your redeemer, who formed you from the womb: I am the Lord, who made all things, I alone stretched out the heavens, and established the earth: who was with me?
44.25: I thwart the predictions of false prophets and make soothsayers rumble; I make the wise retreat and turn their knowledge into folly.
44.26: I fulfill the word of my servant and carry out the counsel of my messengers; I say of Jerusalem, «Let it be inhabited,» and of the cities of Judah, «Let them be rebuilt; I will raise up their ruins.»
44.27: I say to the abyss, «Dry up, I will dry up your rivers.»
44.28: I say of Cyrus, «He is my shepherd; he will accomplish all my will, saying to Jerusalem, »Be rebuilt,’ and to the temple, ‘Be founded.’”

45.1: Thus says the Lord to his anointed, to Cyrus, whose right hand I have taken to subdue nations before him and to loose the belts of kings, to open doors before him so that entrances may not be shut:
45.2: I will go before you, I will make the rough paths straight, I will break down the gates of bronze and I will cut through the bars of iron.
45.3: I will give you the hidden treasures and the buried riches, so that you may know that I am the Lord, the God of Israel, who has called you by your name.
45.4: For the sake of Jacob my servant and Israel my chosen, I have called you by your name, I have designated you when you did not know me.
45.5: I am the Lord and there is no other; besides me there is no God. I armed you when you did not know me,
45.6: so that from the rising of the sun to its setting people may know that there is none besides me. I am the Lord, and there is no other.,
45.7: I form the light and create darkness, I bring peace and create disaster: I, the Lord, do all these things.
45.8: Heavens, pour down your dew from above, and let the clouds rain down righteousness. Let the earth open up and bring forth salvation, and let righteousness spring up with it. I, the Lord, create these things.
45.9: Woe to him who quarrels with his Maker, a vessel among earthen vessels. Will the clay say to him who fashions it, «What are you making?» Will your work say, «He has no hands?»
45.10: Woe to him who says to a father, "Why do you beget?" and to a woman, "Why do you give birth?"«
45.11: Thus says the Lord, the Holy One of Israel and the one who formed him: «Will you dare to question me about the future, to command me concerning my children and the work of my hands?
45.12: It is I who made the earth and created man on it, it is I whose hands stretched out the heavens, I who command all their army.
45.13: It is I who have raised him up in my righteousness and I make all his ways straight. He is the one who will rebuild my city and set my captives free, without ransom or reward, says the Lord of hosts.
45.14: Thus says the Lord: The gains of Egypt and the profits of Ethiopia and the Sabeans of great stature shall come to you and be yours; they shall follow you, they shall pass by in chains and bow down before you, they shall say to you in supplication: «There is no God except with you and there is no other, no other God whatsoever.»
45.15: In truth, you are a hidden God, God of Israel, O Savior.
45.16: They are all ashamed and confused, they go away confused, the idol makers.
45.17: Israel is saved by the Lord with an eternal salvation; you will have neither shame nor confusion in the ages to come.
45.18: For thus says the Lord, who created the heavens, he, the God who fashioned the earth, who perfected and established it, who did not make it a wasteland, but fashioned it to be inhabited: I am the Lord and there is no other.
45.19: I have not spoken in secret, in some dark place of the earth. I have not said to the descendants of Jacob, «Seek me in vain.» I, the Lord, speak what is right; I declare the truth.
45.20: Assemble yourselves and come, draw near together, you survivors of the nations. They know nothing, those who carry their wooden idols and invoke a god who does not save.
45.21: Call them, bring them near, and let them consult together. Who has proclaimed these things from the beginning and declared them long ago? Was it not I, the Lord? And there is no God besides me, I, the righteous God, and there is no savior besides me.
45.22: Turn to me and you will be saved, all you inhabitants of the earth, for I am God and there is no other.
45.23: I have sworn by myself, from my mouth comes the truth, a word which shall not be revoked: Every knee shall bow before me and every tongue shall swear allegiance to me.
45.24: «In the Lord alone,» they will say of me, “do justice and strength reside.” They will come to him, but all those who were incensed against him will be put to shame.
45.25: In the Lord all the race of Israel will be justified and glorified.

46.1: Bel collapses, Nebo sinks, their images are placed on animals, beasts of burden; these idols that you carried are loaded, like a heavy burden, onto weary beasts.
46.2: They sag, they collapse together, they cannot save the burden, they themselves go into captivity.
46.3: Listen to me, house of Jacob and all of you, remnant of the house of Israel, whom I have borne since your birth, whom I carried from your mother's womb.
46.4: Even to your old age I will be the same, even to your gray hairs I will sustain you. I have already done so and I will carry you again, I will sustain you, I will deliver you.
46.5: To whom will you compare me, and to whom will you make me equal? To whom will you assimilate me, that we may be alike?
46.6: They take gold from their purse and weigh silver on the scales, they hire a refiner to make it into a god, and they bow down and worship.
46.7: They carry it on their shoulder, they support it, and they place it where it is, and it stays there without moving. Even when they shout to it, it does not answer; it does not save anyone from distress.
46.8: Think about this and show yourselves men, rebels, take it to heart.,
46.9: Remember the things of olden days. Yes, I am God and there is no other.,
46.10: I am God, and there is none like me: I who from the beginning announce the end, and from ancient times things which are not yet, who say: «My purpose shall stand, and I will do all my will,»
46.11: He who calls the eagle from the East, the man of my purpose from a distant land. I have spoken, I will accomplish; I have resolved, I will execute.
46.12: Listen to me, you stubborn-hearted men, who are far from justice.
46.13: I am bringing my righteousness near, it is not far off, and my salvation will not delay; I will give salvation to Zion, my glory to Israel.

47.1: Come down, sit in the dust, virgin, daughter of Babylon, sit on the ground, without a throne, daughter of the Chaldeans, for you will no longer be called delicate, voluptuous.
47.2: Take the millstone and grind flour, remove your veil, lift up the folds of your robe, bare your legs, to cross the rivers.
47.3: Let your nakedness be exposed, let your shame be seen. I want revenge, I will spare no one.
47.4: Our Redeemer is called the Lord of hosts, the Holy One of Israel.
47.5: Sit in silence, enter into darkness, daughter of the Chaldeans, for you will no longer be called the sovereign of kingdoms.
47.6: I was angry with my people, I let my inheritance be desecrated and I delivered them into your hands. You showed them no mercy, you laid your yoke heavily upon the old man.
47.7: You said, "I am sovereign forever." So you did not heed these things, you did not consider the end of all this.
47.8: And now listen to this, you voluptuary, seated in safety, you who said in your heart: "I and only I. I will never be a widow, nor bereft of my children."«
47.9: These two things will happen to you suddenly, in the same day: the loss of your children and widowhood; they will come upon you in their fullness, despite the multitude of your spells, despite the power of your enchantments.
47.10: You confided in your malice, saying, "No one sees me." Your wisdom and knowledge seduced you, while you said in your heart, "I am, and there is no one but me."«
47.11: And misfortune will come upon you without you being able to avert it, calamity will fall upon you without you being able to turn it away, and ruin will come upon you suddenly without you suspecting it.
47.12: So remain with your enchantments and the multitude of spells you have practiced since your youth. Perhaps you can profit from them, perhaps you will inspire terror.
47.13: You have grown weary with consultations, let them come forward and save you, those who measure the heavens, who observe the stars, who reveal at each new moon what is to befall you.
47.14: Behold, they have become like stubble: the fire will consume them; they will not save their lives from the power of the flame; it is not an ember to warm oneself by, nor a fire to sit before.
47.15: These are the ones for whom you toiled, those with whom you traded from your youth: they flee each in their own direction, there is no one to save you.

48.1: Listen to this, house of Jacob, you who are called by the name of Israel and who came out of the spring of Judah, you who swear by the name of the Lord and who praise the God of Israel, but without sincerity or uprightness.
48.2: For they take their name from the holy city and they rely on the God of Israel, whose name is the Lord of hosts.
48.3: From a long time ago, I announced the first things, they came out of my mouth, I proclaimed them, suddenly I acted and they came to pass.
48.4: Since I knew you were tough, that your neck was like an iron bar and your forehead like brass,
48.5: I told you these things long ago; I declared them to you before they happened, lest you should say, "My idol made them, my god of wood or cast iron commanded them."«
48.6: You have heard it, see, it has all been accomplished, but will you not declare it? Now I am telling you new and hidden things that you do not know.
48.7: They are created now, and not before; until this day you had not heard of them, lest you should say, «Behold, I knew it.»
48.8: You heard nothing of it, you knew nothing of it, your ear never perceived anything of it, because I knew that you are completely unfaithful, that your name is Prevaricator from your mother's womb.
48.9: For the sake of my name, I restrain my anger, and for the sake of my glory, I am patient with you, so as not to destroy you.
48.10: Behold, I have melted you down, but without obtaining silver, I have tested you in the crucible of affliction.
48.11: It is for my own love, for my own love that I do it, for how could my name be profaned? I will not give my glory to another.
48.12: Listen to me, Jacob, and you, Israel, whom I have called, it is I, I who am the first, I also who am the last.
48.13: It is also my hand that laid the foundation of the earth and my right hand that stretched out the heavens; it is I who call them and immediately they appear.
48.14: Assemble all of you and listen: who among them has announced these things? He whom the Lord loves will accomplish his will in Babel, and his arm will be against the Chaldeans.
48.15: I, I have spoken and I have called him, I have brought him here, and his way will be prosperous.
48.16: Come near to me, hear this: From the beginning I have not spoken in secret, since these things have happened, I am here, and now the Lord God sends me with his Spirit.
48.17: Thus says the Lord, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: I, the Lord your God, teach you for your good, I lead you in the way you should walk.
48.18: Oh, pay attention to my commandments and your peace will be like a river, your righteousness like the waves of the sea,
48.19: Your offspring will be like the sand, and the fruit of your womb like the grains of sand; his name will not be cut off or blotted out before me.
48.20: Go out from Babylon, flee from the Chaldeans with shouts of joy. Publish it, proclaim it, make it known to the ends of the earth. Say: «The Lord has redeemed his servant Jacob.”.
48.21: They were not thirsty in the desert where he led them; he made water flow for them from the rock; he split the rock and the water gushed out.»
48.22: There is no peace for the wicked, says the Lord.

49.1: Islands, hear me, distant peoples, pay attention. The Lord called me from the womb, from my mother's womb he proclaimed my name.
49.2: He made my mouth like a sharp sword, he sheltered me in the shadow of his hand, he made me a pointed arrow, he hid me in his quiver.
49.3: And he said to me, «You are my servant, Israel, in whom I will be glorified.»
49.4: And I said, «In vain I have labored, uselessly, for nothing; I have spent my strength, but my right is with the Lord and my reward with my God.»
49.5: And now the Lord speaks, he who formed me from my mother's womb to be his servant, to bring Jacob back to him and to gather Israel to him. And I am honored in the eyes of the Lord, and my God is my strength.
49.6: He said, «It is too small a thing for you to be my servant to restore the tribes of Jacob and to bring back the preserved of Israel; I will also make you a light to the nations, that my salvation may reach to the ends of the earth.»
49.7: Thus says the Lord, the Redeemer and the Holy One of Israel, to him who is despised, abominable to the people, to the slave of tyrants: Kings shall see you and rise up, princes and they shall bow down, because of the Lord who is faithful and the Holy One of Israel who has chosen you.
49.8: Thus says the Lord: In the time of favor I will answer you, and in the day of salvation I will help you; I have kept you and established a covenant for the people, to rebuild the land, to divide the devastated inheritances,
49.9: to say to the captives, «Come out!» and to those in darkness, «Come to the light!» They will graze along the roads, and on every hill will be their pastures.,
49.10: They will not be hungry, they will not be thirsty, nor will the burning sand or the sun reach them. For he who has compassion on them will guide them and lead them to springs of water.
49.11: I will make all my mountains into roads, and my highways will be raised up.
49.12: Here are some who come from afar, here are some from the north and the west, and these are from the land of Sinim.
49.13: Heavens, shout for joy! Earth, tremble! Mountains, burst into song! For the Lord has comforted his people and has compassion on his afflicted.
49.14: Zion said, «The Lord has forsaken me, the Lord has forgotten me.»
49.15: Can a woman forget her nursing child, or show no compassion for the child she has borne? Even if mothers should forget, I will not forget you.
49.16: Look, I have engraved you on the palm of my hands, your walls are always before my eyes.
49.17: Your sons are rushing to you, those who had destroyed and devastated you are turning away from you.
49.18: Lift up your eyes around you and see: they are all gathering together, they are coming to you. As surely as I live, declares the Lord, you will clothe yourself with them all as with an ornament, and you will gird yourself with them as with a bride's sash.
49.19: For your ruins, your deserts, your desolate land, all this will now be too small for your inhabitants, and those who devoured you have departed.
49.20: Then they will say in your ears, your sons, whom you were deprived of: "The space is too small for me, make room for me so that I may live there."«
49.21: And you will say in your heart: «Who bore me these? I was childless, barren, banished and rejected, and these, who raised them? Behold, I was left all alone; where were these?»
49.22: Thus says the Lord God: Behold, I will raise my hand to the nations, and set my banner to the peoples, and they shall bring your sons in their arms and carry your daughters on their shoulders.
49.23: Kings will be your foster fathers and their princesses your nurses; they will bow down before you with their faces to the ground and lick the dust of your feet, and you will know that I am the Lord and that those who hope in me will not be put to shame.
49.24: But will the powerful be able to wrest their prey from them, and will the righteous who have been taken captive escape?
49.25: Thus says the Lord: Even the prey of the mighty will be snatched from him, and the prey of the violent will escape him. Your adversaries, I will fight against them, your children, I will save them.
49.26: I will make your oppressors eat their own flesh, they will be drunk with their own blood as with new wine, and all flesh will know that I, the Lord, am your Savior and that your redeemer is the Mighty One of Jacob.

50.1: Thus says the Lord: Where is your mother’s certificate of divorce, by which I sent her away? Or which of my creditors did I sell you to? It was because of your iniquities that you were sold, because of your sins that your mother was sent away.
50.2: I came: why was there no one? I called: why did no one answer? Is my hand too short to deliver, or do I lack the strength to save? Behold, by my rebuke I dry up the sea, I turn the rivers into a desert; their fish rot for lack of water and perish from thirst.
50.3: I clothe the heavens with darkness and cover them with sackcloth.
50.4: The Lord God has given me the tongue of a disciple, so that I may know how to strengthen the weary with a word. He awakens me morning by morning, he awakens my ear to listen as a disciple.
50.5: The Lord God opened my ear and I did not resist, I did not turn back.
50.6: I offered my back to those who struck me and my cheeks to those who tore out my beard, I did not hide my face from insults and spitting.
50.7: The Lord God came to my aid, therefore insult did not overcome me, therefore I set my face like a flint and I knew that I would not be put to shame.
50.8: He who vindicates me is near: who will contend against me? Let us stand together. Who is my adversary? Let him come near to me.
50.9: The Lord God has come to my aid: who would condemn me? Ah, they will all fall to pieces like a garment, the moth will devour them.
50.10: Who among you fears the Lord and obeys the voice of his Servant? Whoever walks in darkness, without light, let him trust in the name of the Lord and rely on his God.
50.11: But all of you who kindle a fire and arm yourselves with flaming arrows, go into the flames of your fire and into the midst of the arrows you have kindled. It is by my hand that these things will happen to you; you will lie down in pain.

51.1: Hear me, you who pursue righteousness, you who seek the Lord, consider the rock from which you were hewn and the quarry from which you were taken.
51.2: Consider Abraham, your father, and Sarah who bore you; for I called him when he was only one, and I blessed him and multiplied him.
51.3: For the Lord has comforted Zion and all her ruins. He has made her wilderness an Eden and her desolate place a garden of the Lord, where joy and gladness will be found, thanksgiving and the sound of singing.
51.4: Pay attention to my voice, O my people, O my nation, give ear to me, for the law will go out from me, and I will establish my commandment to be a light to the peoples.
51.5: My justice is near, my salvation will appear, and my arm will judge the peoples; the islands hope in me and trust in my arm.
51.6: Lift up your eyes to the heavens and look down to the earth, for the heavens will vanish like smoke and the earth will wear out like a garment, and its inhabitants will perish likewise. But my salvation will last forever, and my righteousness will never fail.
51.7: Hear me, you who know justice, O people who have my law in your heart: Do not fear the insults of men and do not be dismayed by their insults.
51.8: For the moth will eat them up like a garment, and the moth will tear them apart like wool. But my righteousness will endure forever, and my salvation to all ages.
51.9: Awake, awake, put on strength, arm of the Lord. Awake as in days of old, as in ages of long ago. Was it not you who cut Rahab to pieces, who pierced the monster?
51.10: Was it not you who dried up the sea, the waters of the great deep, who made a way out of the depths of the sea, to bring the redeemed through?
51.11: So the redeemed of the Lord will return, they will come to Zion with shouts of joy, eternal gladness will crown their heads, joy and gladness will fill them, sorrow and sighing will flee away.
51.12: It is I, it is I who comfort you. Who are you to fear a mortal man, a son of man who passes away like grass?,
51.13: To forget the Lord, your Creator, who stretched out the heavens and laid the foundations of the earth, and to tremble perpetually, all day long, before the fury of the tyrant, when he prepares to destroy you? And where is the fury of the tyrant?
51.14: Soon the one who is bent will be freed; he will not die in the pit, nor will he lack his bread.
51.15: I am the Lord your God, who stirs up the sea and its waves roar; the Lord of hosts is his name.
51.16: I have put my words in your mouth and covered you with the shadow of my hand, to plant heavens and lay the foundations of the earth, and to say to Zion, «You are my people.»
51.17: Awake, awake, rise up, Jerusalem, you who have drunk from the hand of the Lord the cup of his wrath, you who have drunk, you have emptied the chalice of staggering.
51.18: Not one of the sons she bore guided her. Not one of the sons she raised took her hand.
51.19: These two misfortunes have struck you: who will speak words of pity to you? Devastation and ruin, famine and the sword—how can I comfort you?
51.20: Your sons, exhausted from strength, lie at the corner of every street, like an antelope in the hunter's net, drunk with the fury of the Lord, with the threat of your God.
51.21: Therefore, hear this, you unhappy woman, intoxicated, but not with wine.
51.22: Thus says your Lord God, your God, who defends his people: Behold, I have taken from your hand the cup of staggering, the chalice of my anger; you shall drink them no more.
51.23: I will put them in the hand of your persecutors, those who said to you, "Bow down, let us pass by." And you made your back like the ground, like a street for passersby.

52.1: Awake, awake, put on your strength, Zion. Put on your festive garments, Jerusalem, holy city. For the uncircumcised and the impure will no longer enter you.
52.2: Shake off your dust, rise up, sit down, Jerusalem, loose the chains from your neck, captive, daughter of Zion.
52.3: For thus says the Lord: You were sold for nothing and without money you shall be redeemed.
52.4: For thus says the Lord God: My people once went down to Egypt to sojourn there, and then Assyria oppressed them without cause.
52.5: And now what have I to do here? Oracle of the Lord, since my people have been taken away without right? Their tyrants howl, oracle of the Lord, and continually, all day long, my name is insulted.
52.6: Therefore my people will know my name; yes, they will know on that day that it is I who say, «Here I am.»
52.7: How beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of the messenger who brings good news of peace, who proclaims happiness, who announces salvation, who says to Zion, «Your God reigns!»
52.8: The voice of your watchmen. They raise their voices, they shout together for joy, for they see with their eyes the return of the Lord to Zion.
52.9: Break forth together in shouts of joy, ruins of Jerusalem! For the Lord has comforted his people, he has redeemed Jerusalem.
52.10: The Lord has bared his holy arm, in the sight of all nations and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of our God.
52.11: Depart, depart, go out from there, touch nothing unclean. Go out from the midst of her, purify yourselves, you who carry the vessels of the Lord.
52.12: For you shall not go out in haste, nor shall you flee away, for the Lord goes before you, and the God of Israel is your rearguard.
52.13: Behold, my servant shall prosper, he shall increase, he shall be exalted, and highly exalted.
52.14: Just as many were astonished to see him, so disfigured was he, his appearance no longer that of a man, nor his face that of the children of men,
52.15: Thus he will cause many nations to tremble. Before him kings will shut their mouths, for they will see what they had not been told, and they will understand what they had not heard.

53.1: Who has believed what we have heard, and to whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?
53.2: He grew up before him like a frail sapling, like a shoot out of dry ground; he had no form or beauty to attract us to him, no appearance to excite our love.
53.3: He was despised and rejected by men, a man of sorrows and familiar with pain, like an object from which one veils one's face, held in contempt, we did not value him.
53.4: Truly, it was our illnesses he bore and our pains he took upon himself, and we looked upon him as one punished, struck down by God and humiliated.
53.5: But he was pierced for our transgressions, he was crushed for our iniquities; the punishment that brought us peace was on him, and by his wounds we are healed.
53.6: We were all like sheep going astray, each of us following our own way, and the Lord has laid on him the iniquity of us all.
53.7: He was mistreated, yet he submitted and did not open his mouth; like a lamb led to the slaughter, and like a sheep before its shearers is silent, he did not open his mouth.
53.8: He was taken away by oppression and judgment, and among his contemporaries, who thought that he was cut off from the land of the living, that the plague struck him because of the sins of my people?
53.9: He was assigned a grave with the wicked, and in his death he was with the rich, although he had done no wrong and there was no deceit in his mouth.
53.10: It pleased the Lord to crush him with suffering, but when his soul has offered the atoning sacrifice, he will see his offspring, he will prolong his days, and the Lord's purpose will prosper in his hands.
53.11: Because of the sufferings of his soul, he will see and be satisfied. By his knowledge, the righteous one, my servant, will justify many, and he himself will bear their iniquities.
53.12: Therefore I will give him a portion among the great, and he will divide the spoils with the strong. Because he poured out his soul unto death, and was numbered with the transgressors, and he himself bore the iniquity of many, and he will intercede for sinners.

54.1: Shout for joy, barren woman, who did not bear children; burst into joy and gladness, you who were not in labor, for the children of the desolate woman are more numerous than the children of her who had a husband, says the Lord.
54.2: Enlarge the space of the tent, let the curtains of your dwelling be spread out, do not restrain them, lengthen your cords and strengthen your stakes.
54.3: For you will spread out to the right and to the left, and your descendants will take possession of the nations and populate the desolate cities.
54.4: Do not be afraid, for you will not be put to shame; do not be ashamed, for you will not be disgraced; for you will forget the shame of your youth and you will no longer remember the reproach of your widowhood.
54.5: For your husband is your Creator, the Lord of hosts is his name, and your Redeemer is the Holy One of Israel, he is called the God of all the earth.
54.6: For like a wife forsaken and grieved, the Lord calls you back like a wife from one's youth who has been rejected, says your God.
54.7: For a moment, I abandoned you, but with great mercy I gather you together again.
54.8: In a fit of anger I hid my face from you for a moment, but with eternal love I have compassion on you, says your Redeemer, the Lord.
54.9: It will be for me like the waters of Noah, when I swore that the waters of Noah would never again cover the earth: so I swore that I would not be angry with you anymore, nor would I threaten you.
54.10: Though the mountains be shaken and the hills be removed, yet my love for you will not be shaken nor my covenant of peace be removed, says the Lord, who has compassion on you.
54.11: Unhappy, storm-battered, without consolation, behold, I will lay your stones in antimony and I will found you on sapphires,
54.12: I will make your battlements of rubies, your gates of carbuncles, and your entire enclosure of precious stones.
54.13: All your sons will be disciples of the Lord, your sons will enjoy great peace.
54.14: You will be established in justice, and anguish will be far from you, for you have nothing to fear; terror will not come near you.
54.15: If a league is formed, it will not originate with me; who has conspired against you? He will fall before you.
54.16: Behold, I created the blacksmith, who blows on the burning coals and takes from them the weapon he must work on, and it is I who created the destroyer to destroy it.
54.17: No weapon forged against you will prevail, and you will refute every tongue that rises to contend with you. This is the heritage of the children of the Lord, and this is their righteousness from me, declares the Lord.

55.1: O all you who are thirsty, come to the waters; you who have no money, come, buy grain and eat; come, buy wine and milk without money and without giving anything in exchange.
55.2: Why spend money on what is not bread, your labor on what does not satisfy? Listen to me then, and eat what is good, and let your soul delight in succulent fare.
55.3: Give ear and come to me, listen and let your soul live, and I will make an eternal covenant with you, granting you the sure graces of David.
55.4: Behold, I have appointed him as a witness to the peoples, a prince and ruler of the peoples.
55.5: Behold, you will call the nation you did not know, and the nations that did not know you will run to you, because of the Lord your God and the Holy One of Israel, for he has glorified you.
55.6: Seek the Lord while he may be found; call upon him while he is near.
55.7: Let the wicked forsake their ways and the criminal their thoughts; let them turn to the Lord, and he will have mercy on them, to our God, for he freely pardons.
55.8: For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways, declares the Lord.
55.9: As the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways and my thoughts than your thoughts.
55.10: Just as the rain and the snow come down from heaven and do not return there, without watering the earth and making it sprout, without giving seed to the sower and bread to the eater,
55.11: So it is with my word that goes out from my mouth: it does not return to me empty, but it accomplishes what I intended and achieves the purpose for which I sent it.
55.12: For you will go out full of joy and be led forth in peace; the mountains and hills will burst forth with joy at your sight, and all the trees of the field will applaud.
55.13: Instead of the thornbush, the cypress will rise, and instead of the brier, the myrtle will grow; this will be for the Lord an honor, an everlasting monument that will not be destroyed.

56.1: Thus says the Lord: Observe justice and practice what is right, for my salvation is near and my righteousness will soon be revealed.
56.2: Blessed is the man who does this, and the son of man who follows it, keeping the Sabbath without profaning it, and keeping his hand from doing any harm.
56.3: Let not the foreigner's son, who is devoted to the Lord, say, "The Lord will surely exclude me from his people," and let not the eunuch say, "Behold, I am a dry tree."«
56.4: For thus says the Lord to the eunuchs: To those who keep my Sabbaths, who choose what pleases me, and who hold fast to my covenant,
56.5: I will give them in my house and within my walls a memorial and a name, better than sons and daughters; I will give them an everlasting name, which will not perish.
56.6: And the foreigners, who have joined themselves to the Lord, to serve him and to love his name, to be his servants, all those who keep the Sabbath, without profaning it, and who hold fast to my covenant,
56.7: I will bring them to my holy mountain and give them joy in my house of prayer; their burnt offerings and sacrifices will be accepted on my altar, for my house will be called a house of prayer for all your peoples.,
56.8: the oracle of the Lord God, who gathers the exiles of Israel. I will gather still others to him, along with those already gathered.
56.9: All you animals of the fields, come and devour, and all you beasts of the forest too.
56.10: Israel's guardians are all blind, they know nothing, they are all mute dogs, who cannot bark, they dream, they lie down, they love to sleep.
56.11: They are ravenous dogs, they never know when to stop eating. And they are shepherds, they understand nothing. They all follow their own path, each according to their own gain, from the first to the last.
56.12: «"Come, I'll get some wine and we'll fill ourselves with strong liquor, and tomorrow will be like today, a great, very great day of joy."»

57.1: However, the righteous disappear and no one takes it to heart; the pious are taken away and no one notices that the righteous have been removed from misfortune.
57.2: and that he enters into peace. Those who have followed the right path rest in their beds.
57.3: But you, come here, son of the sorceress, offspring of adulterers and prostitutes.
57.4: Who are you mocking? Against whom do you open your mouth and stick out your tongue? Are you not children of corruption, a race of lies?
57.5: You warm yourselves near sacred trees, under every green tree. You slaughter children in the valleys, under the caves in the rocks?
57.6: Your portion is among the smooth stones of the brook; there, there is your lot. You have even poured libations for them, presented them with offerings. Can I be satisfied with that?
57.7: On a high and lofty mountain you have made your bed. And it is there that you go up to make sacrifices.
57.8: Behind the door and the posts, you have placed your memorial, for, far from me, you uncover your bed, you climb onto it, you widen your bed and you set your wages for them, you love their embraces, you defile your gaze.
57.9: You present yourself before the king with oil, you multiply your perfumes, you send messengers far away, you humble yourself to Sheol.
57.10: Through your efforts, you tire yourself out, but you don't say, "It's in vain." You still find strength in your hand, which is why you don't feel sick.
