57.11: And whom do you fear? Who are you afraid of, that you will be unfaithful to me, that you will no longer remember me, that you will not care about me? Have I not been silent for a long time, while you did not fear me?
57.12: I will proclaim your righteousness and your works which do you no good.
57.13: When you cry out, let them deliver you, your many gods. The wind will carry them all away, a breath will sweep them off. But he who trusts in me will inherit the land and possess my holy mountain.
57.14: And they will say: Clear the way, clear the path, make it straight. Remove every obstacle from the way of my people.
57.15: For thus says the Most High, who dwells in an eternal dwelling and is called the Holy One: I dwell in a high and holy place and at the same time with the contrite and humble man, to give life to the spirit of the humble, to give life to the contrite hearts.
57.16: For I do not want to contend forever, nor hold eternal anger, for before me would fall the mind and the souls that I have made.
57.17: Because of his sinful lust, I became angry and, remaining hidden, I struck him in my wrath, and he, rebellious, followed the way of his own heart.
57.18: I have seen his ways and I will heal him; I will be his guide and I will give him and his afflicted ones comfort.
57.19: He who creates praise on the lips says: «Peace, peace, to those who are far and to those who are near, says the Lord, and I will heal them.»
57.20: But the wicked are like the troubled sea, which cannot calm down and whose waves roll with mud and silt.
57.21: There is no peace, says my God, for the wicked.

58.1: Cry out with all your might, do not hold back, raise your voice like a trumpet and denounce to my people their sin, to the house of Jacob their iniquities.
58.2: They seek me daily and want to know my ways, like a nation that did righteousness and did not forsake the commandment of its God. They ask me for just judgments; they want God to draw near.
58.3: «What good is it for us to fast, if you do not see it, or to humble ourselves, if you do not take heed?» On the day of your fast, you conduct your business and urge all your employees to work.
58.4: Behold, it is in quarreling and fighting that you fast, even to the point of striking your fists wickedly. You do not fast today, so that you may make your voice heard on high.
58.5: Is this the kind of fasting I desire? Is this a day for a man to humble himself? To bow one's head like a reed, to lie in sackcloth and ashes: is this what you call a fast, a day acceptable to the Lord?
58.6: Is not this the fast I have chosen: to loose the chains of injustice, to untie the knots of the yoke, to set the oppressed free, to break every kind of yoke?
58.7: Is it not about breaking your bread with the hungry, taking in the homeless poor, and covering the naked when you see them, and not turning away from your own flesh?
58.8: Then your light will break forth like the dawn, and your healing will quickly spring up; your righteousness will go before you, and the glory of the Lord will be your rearguard.
58.9: Then you will call and the Lord will answer; you will cry out and he will say, «Here I am.» If you remove from your midst the yoke, the pointing of the finger, and slanderous speech,
58.10: If you give your food to the hungry and satisfy the afflicted soul, your light will rise in the darkness and your gloom will shine like midday.
58.11: And the Lord will guide you forever; he will satisfy your soul in parched places. He will give strength to your bones; you will be like a well-watered garden, like a spring of living water that never fails.
58.12: Your children will rebuild your ancient ruins, you will raise up foundations laid in the ages of old, you will be called the repairer of breaches, the restorer of roads, to make the land habitable.
58.13: If you refrain from trampling the Sabbath underfoot, by attending to your own affairs on my holy day, and if you call the Sabbath a delight, venerable is the holy day of the Lord, and honor it by not pursuing your own ways, by not engaging in your own affairs or in idle talk,
58.14: Then you will find your delight in the Lord, and I will carry you off as in triumph to the heights of the land, and I will make you enjoy the inheritance of Jacob your father, for the mouth of the Lord has spoken.

59.1: Behold, the hand of the Lord is not too short to save, nor his ear too dull to hear.
59.2: But your iniquities have put a separation between you and your God; your sins have made him hide his face from you, so that he does not hear you.
59.3: For your hands are stained with blood and your fingers with iniquity, your lips speak lies, your tongue utters perverse speech.
59.4: No one brings a just complaint, no one pleads according to the truth; falsehoods are relied upon and lies are invoked, evil is conceived and crime is born.
59.5: They incubate basilisk eggs and weave spider webs; whoever eats their eggs will die, and if one is crushed, a viper will emerge.
59.6: Their fabrics cannot be used as clothing and one cannot cover oneself with their work; their works are criminal works, a work of violence is in their hands.
59.7: Their feet run towards evil and hasten to shed innocent blood; their thoughts are thoughts of crime; devastation and ruin are in their path.
59.8: They do not know the path of peace, and there is no straightness in their ways; they make crooked paths for themselves: whoever walks in them does not know peace.
59.9: That is why judgment has departed from us and justice has not come to us; we look for light, but behold, darkness; for the brightness of day, but we walk in darkness.
59.10: We grope like blind people along a wall, we grope like people without eyes, we stumble at midday as at dusk, in the midst of vigorous men, we are like the dead.
59.11: We all growl like bears, like doves, we never stop moaning, we await judgment and it does not come, salvation and it remains far from us.
59.12: For our transgressions are many before you, and our sins testify against us; yes, our transgressions are clearly evident to us, and we acknowledge our iniquities:
59.13: To be unfaithful and to deny the Lord, to withdraw from our God, to utter violence and rebellion, to conceive and utter false words from our hearts.
59.14: And justice has withdrawn and righteousness stands far from us, for truth stumbles in the public square and righteousness cannot enter it.,
59.15: Truth has vanished, and whoever turns away from evil is plundered. The Lord has seen it, and it displeases him that there is no more righteousness.
59.16: He saw that there was no one there and was astonished that no one intervened. So his arm came to his aid and his justice was his support.
59.17: He put on justice as a breastplate and the helmet of salvation on his head; he took on vengeance as his garment and wrapped himself in jealousy as in a cloak.
59.18: As the deeds, so the retribution: fury against his adversaries, reprisals against his enemies, he will use reprisals against the islands.
59.19: And they will fear the name of the Lord from the west and his glory from the rising of the sun, for he will come like a narrowed river, rushed by the breath of the Lord.
59.20: He will come as Redeemer for Zion, for those in Jacob who will repent of their iniquities, declares the Lord.
59.21: And I, this is my covenant with them, says the Lord: My spirit which is upon you and my words which I have put in your mouth shall not cease to be in your mouth and in the mouth of your children and in the mouth of your children's children, says the Lord, from now on and forever.

60.1: Arise and shine, for your light has appeared and the glory of the Lord has risen upon you.
60.2: For darkness covers the earth and thick darkness the peoples, but the Lord rises upon you and his glory appears upon you.
60.3: Nations walk towards your light, and kings towards the brightness of your dawn.
60.4: Lift up your eyes around you and see: They all gather together, they come to you, your sons come from afar and your daughters are carried on their arms.
60.5: Then you will see it and you will be radiant, your heart will thrill and swell with joy, for the riches of the sea will come to you, the treasures of the nations will come to you.
60.6: Multitudes of camels will cover you, the dromedaries of Midian and Ephah, all those from Sheba will come, they will bring gold and frankincense and proclaim the praises of the Lord.
60.7: All the flocks of Cedar will be gathered to you, the rams of Nebaioth will serve you, they will come up on my altar as a pleasing offering, and I will glorify the house of my glory.
60.8: Who are these that fly like clouds, like doves to their dovecote?
60.9: For the islands will wait for me, and the ships of Tarshish will come first, to bring your sons from afar, with their silver and gold, to honor the name of the Lord your God, the Holy One of Israel, because he has glorified you.
60.10: Foreigners will rebuild your walls and their kings will be your servants, for I struck you in my anger, but in my favor I had compassion on you.
60.11: Your gates will always be open, day and night; they will never be shut, so that the treasures of the nations and their kings may be brought into you in triumphal procession.
60.12: For the nation and the kingdom that will not serve you will perish; those nations will be utterly destroyed.
60.13: The glory of Lebanon will come to you, the cypress, the plane tree and the box tree all together, to adorn the place of my sanctuary, and I will glorify the place where my feet rest.
60.14: The sons of your oppressors will come to you with bowed heads, and all who despised you will prostrate themselves at your feet, and you will be called the City of the Lord, the Zion of the Holy One of Israel.
60.15: Instead of you being neglected, hated and lonely, I will make you the pride of the ages, the joy of all generations.
60.16: You will suckle the milk of nations, you will gorge yourself on the riches of kings, and you will know that I, the Lord, am your Savior and that the Mighty One of Jacob is your Redeemer.
60.17: Instead of bronze, I will bring gold, and instead of iron, I will bring silver, and instead of wood, bronze, and instead of stones, iron; and I will give you peace as rulers, and justice as magistrates.
60.18: Violence will no longer be heard in your land, nor devastation or ruin within your borders; you will call your walls: Salvation and your gates: Praise.
60.19: The sun will no longer be your light by day, nor will the moonlight shine on you, but the Lord will be your eternal light, and your God will be your glory.
60.20: Your sun will never set again, and your moon will never wane, for the Lord will be your everlasting light, and your days of mourning will be over.
60.21: In your people, all will be righteous and they will possess the land forever, they, the shoot I have planted, the work of my hands, created for my glory.
60.22: The smallest will become a thousand, and the least a mighty nation. I, the Lord, will hasten these things in their time.

61.1: The Spirit of the Lord God is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me; he has sent me to bring good news to the poor, to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives and release to the prisoners,
61.2: to proclaim a year of favor to the Lord and a day of vengeance to our God, to comfort all who mourn,
61.3: to bring to the afflicted of Zion and to clothe them with a diadem instead of ashes, the oil of gladness instead of mourning, a garment of celebration instead of a faint spirit; they shall be called holy trees of righteousness, plantings of the Lord for his glory.
61.4: They will rebuild the ancient ruins, they will raise up the rubble of the past, they will restore the destroyed cities, the ruins of ages gone by.
61.5: Foreigners will be there to tend your flocks, and foreigners will be your farmers and vinedressers.
61.6: But you will be called priests of the Lord, you will be named «ministers of our God.» You will eat the wealth of the nations and adorn yourselves with their splendor.
61.7: Instead of your shame, you will have a double portion, and instead of disgrace, they will rejoice in their inheritance; they will possess a double portion in their land, and there will be eternal joy for them.
61.8: For I, the Lord, love justice, I hate plunder done by treachery; I will faithfully pay them their reward and I will make an everlasting covenant with them.
61.9: Their descendants will be renowned among the nations, and their offspring among the peoples; all who see them will acknowledge that they are a race blessed by the Lord.
61.10: I will be filled with gladness in the Lord and my heart will rejoice in my God, because he has clothed me with the garments of salvation and covered me with the robe of righteousness, as a bridegroom adorns his head with a diadem, as a bride adorns herself with her jewels.
61.11: For as the earth brings forth its shoots and as a garden causes its seeds to sprout, so the Lord God will cause righteousness and praise to spring up before all nations.

62.1: For Zion’s sake I will not keep silent, and for Jerusalem’s sake I will not remain quiet, till her righteousness breaks forth like the dawn and her salvation shines like a torch.
62.2: The nations will see your righteousness and all kings your glory, and you will be called by a new name, which the mouth of the Lord will choose.
62.3: You will be a crown of honor in the hand of the Lord, a royal tiara in the hand of our God.
62.4: You will no longer be called Forsaken, nor will your land be called Desolate. But you will be called My-Delight-in-Her and your land Married, for the Lord will take delight in you, and your land will have a husband.
62.5: As a young man marries a virgin, so your sons will marry you; and as the bridegroom rejoices over the bridegroom, so your God will rejoice over you.
62.6: On your walls, Jerusalem, I have placed watchmen; they will never be silent, day or night. O you who remember the Lord, do not rest,
62.7: and give him no rest until he restores Jerusalem and makes it the praise of the earth.
62.8: The Lord has sworn by his right hand and by his mighty arm: I will no longer give your wheat as food to your enemies, and foreigners will no longer drink your wine, the fruit of your labors.
62.9: But those who have reaped the harvest will eat it and praise the Lord, and those who have gathered the vintage will drink it in the courts of my sanctuary.
62.10: Go through, go through the gates, make straight the way for the people. Clear, clear the road, remove the stones, raise a banner over the peoples.
62.11: This is what the Lord has proclaimed to the ends of the earth: Say to the daughter of Zion, «Behold, your Savior is coming; behold, his reward is with him, and his recompense precedes him.»
62.12: And they will be called the Holy People, the Redeemed of the Lord, and you will be called Sought After, the City Not Forsaken.

63.1: Who is this who comes from Edom, from Bozrah, in scarlet robes? He is magnificent in his clothing, he strides forward in the greatness of his strength. It is I, who speak in righteousness and am mighty to save.
63.2: Why is there red on your garment and are your clothes like those of the presser?
63.3: I trod the winepress alone, and among the peoples no one was with me. And I trampled them in my anger, trampled them in my fury; their juice splashed on my garments, and I soiled all my clothing.
63.4: For a day of vengeance was in my heart, and the year of my redemption had come.
63.5: I looked around and there was no one to help me, I was astonished and there was no one to support me. So my arm saved me and my fury sustained me.
63.6: I crushed the peoples in my anger, I intoxicated them with my fury, and I made their blood flow on the ground.»
63.7: I will celebrate the mercies of the Lord, the praises of the Lord, according to all that the Lord has done for us, and his great goodness to the house of Israel, which he has shown them in his compassion and his immense mercy.
63.8: He said, "Yes, they are my people, sons who will not be unfaithful," and he became their savior.
63.9: In all their distress he was distressed, and the angel of his presence saved them; in his love and compassion he redeemed them; he sustained and carried them all the days of old.
63.10: But they rebelled and grieved his Holy Spirit, so he turned himself into an enemy to them and waged war against them.
63.11: Then his people remembered the days of old, the days of Moses. Where is he who brought up the shepherd of his flock from the sea? Where is he who placed his Holy Spirit among them?,
63.12: who led his glorious arm to the right hand of Moses, who divided the waters before them, to make for himself an everlasting name,
63.13: which made them walk through the abyss, without stumbling, like a horse in the steppe,
63.14: like cattle going down into the valley? The Spirit of the Lord led them to rest: this is how you guided your people, to make a glorious name for yourselves.
63.15: Look down from heaven and see, from your holy and magnificent dwelling: Where are your zeal and your power, the yearning of your heart and your compassion? For me, they have ceased.
63.16: For you are our father, for Abraham does not know us and Israel does not acknowledge us. You, the Lord, are our father, our Redeemer: this is your name from of old.
63.17: Why, O Lord, would you make us wander from your ways, would you harden our hearts against your fear? Return, for the sake of your servants and the tribes of your inheritance.
63.18: Your holy people possessed the land for a very short time; our enemies trampled your sanctuary underfoot.
63.19: We have long been like a people you do not rule, over whom your name is not invoked. Oh, if you would rend the heavens, if you would come down, the mountains would tremble before you.

64.1: like a fire that kindles dry wood, like a fire that makes water boil, to reveal your name to your adversaries, so that the nations tremble before you,
64.2: By doing terrible, unexpected things, you would descend, the mountains would tremble before you.
64.3: of which no one had ever heard. No one has ever heard, no eye has seen a God other than you act thus for those who hope in him.
64.4: You come to meet those who joyfully do what is right, those who walk in your ways and remember you. Behold, you were angry and we were guilty, and this has been so for a long time: shall we be saved?
64.5: We were all like an unclean man, and all our righteous acts were like filthy rags. We all faded like a leaf, and our iniquities swept us away like the wind.
64.6: There was no one who called on your name, who awoke to cling to you. For you had hidden your face from us and you let us perish in our iniquities.
64.7: And now, O Lord, you are our father, we are the clay and you are the one who formed us, we are all the work of your hand.
64.8: Do not be extremely angry, O Lord, and do not remember iniquity forever. Look then: we are all your people.
64.9: Your holy cities have become a desert, Zion has become a desert, Jerusalem a desolate place.
64.10: Our holy and glorious house, where our fathers celebrated your praises, has become prey to the flames and all that was dear to us has been devastated.
64.11: In the face of these evils, will you restrain yourself, O Lord? Will you remain silent and afflict us excessively?

65.1: I let myself be sought by those who did not ask for me, I let myself be found by those who did not seek me, I said: "Here I am, here I am." to a nation that did not bear my name.
65.2: All day long I stretched out my hands toward a rebellious people, toward those who walk in the evil way, according to their own thoughts,
65.3: towards a people who provoked me, facing me, constantly, sacrificing in the gardens, burning incense on bricks,
65.4: staying in tombs and spending the night in hiding places, eating pork and unclean foods in their dishes,
65.5: saying, «Get away. Do not come near me, for I am holy to you.» Those are like smoke in my nostrils, a fire that burns continually.
65.6: Behold, it is written before me: I will not remain silent until I have repaid, repaid within their midst
65.7: your iniquities, with the iniquities of your fathers, says the Lord, who burned incense on the mountains and insulted me on the hills, I will measure out to them in their bosom the wages of their former conduct.
65.8: Thus says the Lord: Just as, finding juice in a bunch of grapes, one says, «Do not destroy it, for there is a blessing in it,» so shall I act for the sake of my servants, so that I will not destroy everything.
65.9: I will bring forth offspring from Jacob and from Judah an heir of my mountains; my chosen ones will possess them, and my servants will dwell there.
65.10: And Sharon will serve as a sheepfold and the valley of Achor as a pasture for cattle, for my people who have sought me.
65.11: But you who have forsaken the Lord, forgotten my holy mountain, who set a table for Gad and fill a bowl for Meni,
65.12: I destine you for the sword, and you will all bow down to be slaughtered. For I called, and you did not answer; I spoke, and you did not listen, but you did what was evil in my sight and chose what I did not want.
65.13: Therefore this is what the Lord God says: “My servants will eat, but you will go hungry; my servants will drink, but you will go thirsty; my servants will rejoice, but you will be ashamed.”,
65.14: Behold, my servants will sing with joy in their hearts, and you will cry out in the pain of your hearts and howl in the anguish of your spirits.,
65.15: And you will leave your name as a curse to my chosen ones, and the Lord God will cause you to perish, but he will call his servants by another name.
65.16: Whoever wishes to be blessed on earth will wish to be blessed by the God of truth, and whoever swears an oath on earth will swear by the God of truth. For the previous anxieties will be forgotten and will have vanished from my sight.
65.17: For behold, I create new heavens and a new earth; the former things shall not be remembered, nor shall they come to mind.
65.18: Rather, rejoice and be ever glad in what I am creating: for behold, I am creating Jerusalem for joy and its people for gladness.
65.19: And I will rejoice over Jerusalem and be glad over my people. And the sound of weeping and the cry of anguish will no longer be heard in it.
65.20: There will no longer be any child born for a few days, nor any old man who does not fulfill the number of his days, for to die a hundred years old will be to die young, and it is at a hundred years that the curse will reach the sinner.
65.21: They will build houses and live in them, they will plant vineyards and eat their fruit.
65.22: They will not build for another to inhabit, nor will they plant for another to eat. For the days of my people will be like the days of the trees, and my chosen ones will wear out the work of their hands.
65.23: They will no longer labor in vain, nor will they bear children for sudden death, for they will be a race blessed by the Lord, and with them their offspring.
65.24: Before they call, I will answer; they will speak again, and I will grant their wish.
65.25: The wolf and the lamb will graze together, the serpent will feed on dust, the lion will eat straw like the ox, and the serpent will feed on dirt. They will neither harm nor destroy on all my holy mountain, says the Lord.

66.1: Thus says the Lord: Heaven is my throne and the earth is my footstool: what kind of house would you build for me, and what would be the place of my rest?
66.2: All these things my hand made, and all these things came into being, declares the Lord. This is the one I look upon: the humble, the brokenhearted, and he trembles at my word.
66.3: He who slaughters an ox kills a man, he who sacrifices a sheep slaughters a dog, he who presents a grain offering offers pig's blood, he who burns incense blesses an idol. They choose their ways and their souls delight in their abominations,
66.4: I too will choose their misfortune and bring upon them what they fear, because I called and no one answered, I spoke and they did not listen, they did what was evil in my sight and they chose what displeased me.
66.5: Listen to the word of the Lord, you who tremble at his word: Your brothers who hate you and reject you because of my name have said, «Let the Lord show his glory, so that we may see your joy.» But they will be put to shame.
66.6: A crash, a tumult rises from the city, a shout from the temple. It is the voice of the Lord, who repays his enemies what they deserve.
66.7: Before she went into labor, she gave birth; before the pains came to her, she brought forth a male child.
66.8: Who has ever heard of such a thing, who has ever seen anything like it? Is a country born in a day, is a nation brought forth in a single moment, that Zion, scarcely in labor, has given birth to her children?
66.9: Shall I open the womb and not bring forth? says the Lord, or shall I, who bring forth, close the womb? says your God.
66.10: Rejoice with Jerusalem and be glad for her, all you who love her. Rejoice greatly with her, all you who mourned over her,
66.11: so that you may be nursed and filled with his comforts, so that you may enjoy with delight the fullness of his glory.
66.12: For thus says the Lord: I will cause peace to flow upon her like a river and the glory of the nations like an overflowing stream; and you shall be nursed, carried on the breast, and caressed on the knees.
66.13: As a man whom his mother comforts, so I will comfort you, and you will be comforted in Jerusalem.
66.14: You will see it, and your heart will rejoice, and your bones will flourish like grass. And the hand of the Lord will be made known to his servants, and his wrath to his enemies.
66.15: For behold, the Lord is coming in fire, and his chariot is like a hurricane, to pour out his wrath in blazing flames and his threats in flames of fire.
66.16: For the Lord executes judgment by fire and by his sword against all flesh, and many will be those whom the Lord will pierce.
66.17: Those who consecrate and purify themselves to go into the gardens, following the one who stands in the middle, those who eat pork, abominable foods, and mice, will all perish together, declares the Lord.,
66.18: I know their works and their thoughts. The time has come to gather all nations and all languages. They will come and see my glory,
66.19: and I will perform a wonder among them. And I will send some of their survivors to the nations, to Tarshish, to Phul and Lud, who draw the bow, to Tubal and Javan, to the distant islands, who have never heard of me or seen my glory, and they will proclaim my glory among the nations.
66.20: And they shall bring all your brothers from among all the nations, as an offering to the Lord, on horses, on chariots, in litters, on mules and camels, to my holy mountain, to Jerusalem, says the Lord, as the children of Israel bring the offering in a pure vessel to the house of the Lord.
66.21: And I will even take some of them as priests and Levites, says the Lord.
66.22: For as the new heavens and the new earth, which I am going to create, will remain before me, declares the Lord, so will your offspring and your name remain.
66.23: From new moon to new moon and from sabbath to sabbath, all flesh shall come to bow down before me, says the Lord.
66.24: And when they go out, they will see the corpses of the men who rebelled against me, for their worm will not die and their fire will not be quenched, and they will be an abomination to all flesh.
